title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong>What the Role Is</strong></p> <p>The Data team at Babylist powers decision-making across the entire business — from product growth to operations to AI. This is a senior individual contributor role on the Data Engineering team, sitting at the intersection of platform thinking and AI-native tooling. You'll own the architecture and systems that make data engineering itself more scalable — not just the pipelines, but the harnesses and agentic scaffolding that generate, test, and maintain them. You'll work cross-functionally with analysts, data scientists, and product teams to keep Babylist's data infrastructure reliable and ahead of where the business is going.</p> <p><strong>Who You Are</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experienced building production AI/LLM systems — RAG pipelines, agentic workflows, tool integrations (e.g., MCP servers) that real users depend on, not just prototypes</li> <li>Platform-minded — you see repetitive data engineering work as a system design problem and build the scaffolding to automate it</li> <li>Deeply fluent in Python and production-grade data engineering, with 7+ years building systems that hold up at scale</li> <li>Proficient with Airflow and dbt — you understand data modeling and ETL principles, not just the tooling</li> <li>Comfortable in AWS — you've provisioned and managed cloud data resources across EC2, S3, Lambda, and EKS</li> <li>Familiar with Snowflake or comparable modern cloud data warehouses</li> <li>Able to work cross-functionally with analysts and data scientists — translating their needs into infrastructure that actually serves them</li> <li>You naturally reach for AI in your work — at Babylist, every team uses AI daily. You're already using it to move faster and improve your output, and you stay curious about what's coming next.</li> <li><strong>You're genuinely excited about what AI can do -&nbsp;</strong>not just as a concept, but as something you want to get your hands on. At Babylist, every team uses AI daily, and we're looking for people who lean in.</li> </ul> <p><strong>How You Will Make An Impact</strong></p> <ul> <li>Build and scale data pipelines for ingestion into Snowflake — with a focus on reliability and performance across a $750M+ e-commerce business</li> <li>Design and ship the agentic systems that perform data engineering work — pipeline generation, testing, and maintenance as automated systems, not one-off builds</li> <li>Develop and maintain ML pipelines that help data scientists operationalize models and integrate them with our data infrastructure</li> <li>Implement and improve data monitoring across complex, multi-system user journeys</li> <li>Collaborate with Analytics Engineers on data modeling and the reliability of shared data assets</li> <li>Partner with product, analyst, and ML teams to deliver end-to-end data solutions — from ingestion to advanced analytics and AI</li> </ul> <p><strong>Why This Role</strong></p> <ul> <li>You're building the meta-layer — not just pipelines, but the systems that build pipelines; it's a rare scope for a data engineering role</li> <li>The data infrastructure is solid; you're extending and automating something that works, not inheriting a mess</li> <li>AI is core to the mandate here, not a future aspiration — agentic data engineering is a real part of the roadmap, not a stretch goal</li> <li>You'll have direct influence on how a 9M+ user platform scales its data systems through a pre-IPO growth moment.</li> </ul> <p><strong>About Compensation</strong></p> <p>We use a market-based approach to compensation. The starting salary range for this role is:</p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;"><strong>$186,086.000 - $221,800 + target 15% annual bonus</strong></span></p> <p>Your starting salary will be based on your location, experience, and qualifications, with increases over time tied to performance, role growth, and internal pay equity.</p> <p><strong>Who We Are</strong></p> <p>Babylist is the leading platform for expecting and new families. More than 10 million people shop with Babylist every year, making it the go-to destination for seamless purchasing, guidance, and expert recommendations. As a modern, AI-forward tech company, Babylist has expanded from a universal registry into a full ecosystem — the Babylist Shop, Babylist Health, Babylist Money, NYC and LA showrooms, branded content, and more — generating $750M in revenue in 2025. Building the generational brand in baby, Babylist is reshaping the $235B kids and baby market and helping parents feel confident, connected, and cared for at every step.</p> <p><strong>Our Ways of Working</strong></p> <p>Babylist is remote-first with team members across the U.S. and Canada who move fast, think smart, and use AI as part of how they work every day — not as an experiment, as an expectation. We come together twice a year to build the relationships behind the work, and we hire people who are genuinely excited about what's possible and prove it through how they show up.</p> <p><strong>Why You Will Love Working At Babylist</strong></p> <p><strong>Our Culture</strong></p> <ul> <li>We work with focus and intention, then step away to recharge</li> <li>We believe in exceptional management, and we invest in the tools and the time to connect with colleagues</li> <li>We build products that positively impact millions of people's lives</li> <li>AI is part of how we work, not a side experiment. You'll have leading tools like Claude and ChatGPT from day one, with no waiting on access, so the busywork gets handled and your judgment goes where it counts</li> </ul> <p><strong>Growth &amp; Development</strong></p> <ul> <li>Competitive pay and meaningful opportunities for career advancement</li> <li>We believe technology and data can solve hard problems</li> <li>We're committed to career progression and performance-based advancement</li> </ul> <p><strong>Benefits</strong></p> <ul> <li>Company-paid medical, dental, and vision insurance</li> <li>Retirement savings plan with company matching and flexible spending accounts</li> <li>Generous paid parental leave and PTO</li> <li>Winter Wonder Week: the whole company takes a paid week off at the end of the year, whether or not you celebrate anything, so everyone's out at once and comes back refreshed</li> <li>Remote work stipend to set up your office</li> <li>Perks for physical, mental, and emotional health, parenting, childcare, and financial planning</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Important Notices</strong></h3> <p><strong>Recorded Interviews. </strong>Babylist uses an interview recording tool to record and transcribe interviews for evaluation purposes in accordance with applicable privacy laws. By participating in an interview, you consent to this recording and transcription.</p> <p><strong>Interview Integrity. </strong>AI is part of how we work at Babylist — we expect you to use it too. Your application and interviews should still reflect you and your own thinking. We'll tell you when AI is encouraged. Misrepresentation at any stage may result in removal from consideration for this and future roles.</p> <p><strong>How We Review Applications </strong>We use AI throughout our hiring process to help review, screen, and assess applications. These tools support our team's review; they do not replace it, and every hiring decision is made by a person.</p> <p><strong>Connections at Babylist. </strong>If you have a family member or close personal relationship with a current Babylist employee, please let your recruiter know. This helps us keep our process fair and transparent for everyone.</p> <p><strong>Protect Yourself from Scams. </strong>All official outreach comes from the Babylist Talent Team via @babylist.com email addresses only. We will never ask for payment or personal financial information. If you receive outreach via WhatsApp, Telegram, or a non-Babylist email — it's not us. Verify open roles at babylist.com/careers.</p>

title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong>What the Role Is</strong></p> <p>The Data team at Babylist powers decision-making across the entire business — from product growth to operations to AI. This is a senior individual contributor role on the Data Engineering team, sitting at the intersection of platform thinking and AI-native tooling. You'll own the architecture and systems that make data engineering itself more scalable — not just the pipelines, but the harnesses and agentic scaffolding that generate, test, and maintain them. You'll work cross-functionally with analysts, data scientists, and product teams to keep Babylist's data infrastructure reliable and ahead of where the business is going.</p> <p><strong>Who You Are</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experienced building production AI/LLM systems — RAG pipelines, agentic workflows, tool integrations (e.g., MCP servers) that real users depend on, not just prototypes</li> <li>Platform-minded — you see repetitive data engineering work as a system design problem and build the scaffolding to automate it</li> <li>Deeply fluent in Python and production-grade data engineering, with 7+ years building systems that hold up at scale</li> <li>Proficient with Airflow and dbt — you understand data modeling and ETL principles, not just the tooling</li> <li>Comfortable in AWS — you've provisioned and managed cloud data resources across EC2, S3, Lambda, and EKS</li> <li>Familiar with Snowflake or comparable modern cloud data warehouses</li> <li>Able to work cross-functionally with analysts and data scientists — translating their needs into infrastructure that actually serves them</li> <li>You naturally reach for AI in your work — at Babylist, every team uses AI daily. You're already using it to move faster and improve your output, and you stay curious about what's coming next.</li> <li><strong>You're genuinely excited about what AI can do -&nbsp;</strong>not just as a concept, but as something you want to get your hands on. At Babylist, every team uses AI daily, and we're looking for people who lean in.</li> </ul> <p><strong>How You Will Make An Impact</strong></p> <ul> <li>Build and scale data pipelines for ingestion into Snowflake — with a focus on reliability and performance across a $750M+ e-commerce business</li> <li>Design and ship the agentic systems that perform data engineering work — pipeline generation, testing, and maintenance as automated systems, not one-off builds</li> <li>Develop and maintain ML pipelines that help data scientists operationalize models and integrate them with our data infrastructure</li> <li>Implement and improve data monitoring across complex, multi-system user journeys</li> <li>Collaborate with Analytics Engineers on data modeling and the reliability of shared data assets</li> <li>Partner with product, analyst, and ML teams to deliver end-to-end data solutions — from ingestion to advanced analytics and AI</li> </ul> <p><strong>Why This Role</strong></p> <ul> <li>You're building the meta-layer — not just pipelines, but the systems that build pipelines; it's a rare scope for a data engineering role</li> <li>The data infrastructure is solid; you're extending and automating something that works, not inheriting a mess</li> <li>AI is core to the mandate here, not a future aspiration — agentic data engineering is a real part of the roadmap, not a stretch goal</li> <li>You'll have direct influence on how a 9M+ user platform scales its data systems through a pre-IPO growth moment.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who We Are</strong></p> <p>Babylist is the leading platform for expecting and new families. More than 10 million people shop with Babylist every year, making it the go-to destination for seamless purchasing, guidance, and expert recommendations. As a modern, AI-forward tech company, Babylist has expanded from a universal registry into a full ecosystem — the Babylist Shop, Babylist Health, Babylist Money, NYC and LA showrooms, branded content, and more — generating $750M in revenue in 2025. Building the generational brand in baby, Babylist is reshaping the $235B kids and baby market and helping parents feel confident, connected, and cared for at every step.</p> <p><strong>Our Ways of Working</strong></p> <p>Babylist is remote-first with team members across the U.S. and Canada who move fast, think smart, and use AI as part of how they work every day — not as an experiment, as an expectation. We come together twice a year to build the relationships behind the work, and we hire people who are genuinely excited about what's possible and prove it through how they show up.</p> <p><strong>Why You Will Love Working At Babylist</strong></p> <p><strong>Our Culture</strong></p> <ul> <li>We work with focus and intention, then step away to recharge</li> <li>We believe in exceptional management, and we invest in the tools and the time to connect with colleagues</li> <li>We build products that positively impact millions of people's lives</li> <li>AI is part of how we work, not a side experiment. You'll have leading tools like Claude and ChatGPT from day one, with no waiting on access, so the busywork gets handled and your judgment goes where it counts</li> </ul> <p><strong>Growth &amp; Development</strong></p> <ul> <li>Competitive pay and meaningful opportunities for career advancement</li> <li>We believe technology and data can solve hard problems</li> <li>We're committed to career progression and performance-based advancement</li> </ul> <p><strong>Benefits</strong></p> <ul> <li>Company-paid medical, dental, and vision insurance</li> <li>Retirement savings plan with company matching and flexible spending accounts</li> <li>Generous paid parental leave and PTO</li> <li>Winter Wonder Week: the whole company takes a paid week off at the end of the year, whether or not you celebrate anything, so everyone's out at once and comes back refreshed</li> <li>Remote work stipend to set up your office</li> <li>Perks for physical, mental, and emotional health, parenting, childcare, and financial planning</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Important Notices</strong></h3> <p><strong>Recorded Interviews. </strong>Babylist uses an interview recording tool to record and transcribe interviews for evaluation purposes in accordance with applicable privacy laws. title: Senior Analytics Engineer, GTM
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>We’re in an unbelievably exciting area of tech and are fundamentally reshaping the data storage industry. Here, you lead with innovative thinking, grow along with us, and join the smartest team in the industry.</p> <p>This type of work—work that changes the world—is what the tech industry was founded on. So, if you're ready to seize the endless opportunities and leave your mark, come join us.</p></div><p><strong>THE ROLE</strong></p> <h2>SHOULD YOU ACCEPT THIS CHALLENGE…</h2> <p>The GTM BI team at Pure Storage supports Revenue Operations across pipeline, forecasting, seller performance, and executive reporting — and we are at an inflection point.</p> <p>We are midway through a strategic migration to dbt as our transformation standard. Beyond that, our roadmap includes scalable automation frameworks, semantic business intelligence, and AI-enabled workflows that help surface insights with less manual effort.</p> <p>As a Senior Analytics Engineer, you will help drive that journey end-to-end. This role is grounded in modernization first: building scalable dbt foundations, improving quality and engineering standards, and enabling the team to operate more efficiently before scaling broader AI-enabled capabilities.</p> <p>You will partner closely with BI analysts, Sales Operations, Systems teams, and backend engineering partners to modernize how analytics is engineered, governed, and consumed across the organization. This role is for someone who wants to build — not maintain.</p> <h2>WHAT YOU’LL DO…</h2> <ul> <li>Lead and contribute hands-on to the team’s dbt and Snowflake modernization initiatives</li> <li>Build scalable semantic and metrics layers supporting GTM domains including pipeline, forecasting, quota, attainment, and seller performance</li> <li>Design and maintain robust ELT/ETL pipelines, orchestration workflows, and reusable business-facing datasets</li> <li>Build CI/CD, testing, observability, lineage, and data quality frameworks for analytics engineering workflows</li> <li>Ingest and transform data from APIs and GTM systems into curated canonical datasets with strong quality standards</li> <li>Drive architecture decisions and establish modern analytics engineering best practices across the team</li> <li>Partner closely with BIAs, RevOps, and Systems teams to translate business problems into scalable data products</li> <li>Help automate manual reporting and operational workflows through configurable and scalable frameworks</li> <li>Apply AI capabilities to workflow automation, operational efficiency, and insight generation, while helping lay the foundation for future conversational analytics initiatives</li> </ul> <h2>WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO BRING TO THIS ROLE…</h2> <ul> <li>7+ years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, or business intelligence</li> <li>Strong expertise in SQL, Python, Snowflake, and modern ELT development practices</li> <li>Hands-on experience with dbt, orchestration workflows, and scalable data modeling</li> <li>Experience implementing CI/CD, automated testing, observability, and data quality frameworks</li> <li>Strong understanding of semantic modeling, metrics layers, governed metrics, and reusable analytics datasets</li> <li>Experience using AI to build workflows, automate processes, improve operational efficiency, or support business intelligence use cases</li> <li>Ability to influence technical direction while remaining hands-on</li> <li>Strong communication and collaboration skills across technical and business teams</li> <li>Pragmatic interest in applying AI capabilities to real business workflows over time</li> <li>Passion for modernization, reporting trust, and scalable analytics engineering</li> </ul> <h2>PREFERRED EXPERIENCE</h2> <ul> <li>GTM, Sales, Revenue Operations, or forecasting analytics domains</li> <li>Salesforce, Clari, Anaplan, Workday, or related enterprise systems</li> <li>Analytics engineering modernization initiatives</li> <li>Self-service analytics ecosystems and semantic layers</li> <li>Conversational analytics, AI copilots, or workflow automation concepts</li> <li>Experience migrating legacy SQL or ETL frameworks into modern dbt-based transformation workflows</li> <li>Tableau or modern BI visualization platforms</li> </ul> <p>#LI-ONSITE<br><br></p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM US:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Innovation</strong>: We celebrate those who think critically, like a challenge, and aspire to be trailblazers.</li> <li><strong>Growth</strong>: We give you the space and support to grow along with us and to contribute to something meaningful. We have been named Fortune's Best Workplaces in Technology™, Fortune's Best Workplaces in the Bay Area™, and certified as a Great Place to Work®!</li> <li><strong>Team</strong>: We build each other up and set aside ego for the greater good.</li> </ul> <p>And because we understand the value of bringing your full and best self to work, we offer a variety of perks to manage a healthy balance, including flexible time off, wellness resources, and company-sponsored team events. Check out <a href="http://purebenefits.com">purebenefits.com</a> for more information.</p> <p><strong>ACCOMMODATIONS AND ACCESSIBILITY:</strong></p> <p>Candidates with disabilities may request accommodations for all aspects of our hiring process. For more on this, contact us at <a href="mailto:TA-Ops@purestorage.com">TA-Ops@purestorage.com</a> if you’re invited to an interview.</p> <p><strong>OUR COMMITMENT TO A STRONG AND INCLUSIVE TEAM:</strong></p> <p>We’re forging a future where everyone finds their rightful place and where every voice matters. Where uniqueness isn’t just accepted but embraced. That’s why we are committed to fostering the growth and development of every person, cultivating a sense of community through our Employee Resource Groups and advocating for inclusive leadership.</p> <p>Everpure is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, transgender status, sexual stereotypes, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or any other characteristic legally protected by the laws of the jurisdiction in which you are being considered for hire.</p> <p style="text-align: center;"><strong>Join us and bring your best.</strong></p> <p style="text-align: center;"><strong>Bring your bold.</strong></p> <p style="text-align: center;"><strong>Pure and simple.</strong></p></div>

title: Senior Data Engineering Manager -  Conversational AI & Finance Control Tower
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>We’re in an unbelievably exciting area of tech and are fundamentally reshaping the data storage industry. Check out <a href="http://purebenefits.com">purebenefits.com</a> for more information.</p> <p><strong>ACCOMMODATIONS AND ACCESSIBILITY:</strong></p> <p>Candidates with disabilities may request accommodations for all aspects of our hiring process. For more on this, contact us at <a href="mailto:TA-Ops@purestorage.com">TA-Ops@purestorage.com</a> if you’re invited to an interview.</p> <p><strong>OUR COMMITMENT TO A STRONG AND INCLUSIVE TEAM:</strong></p> <p>We’re forging a future where everyone finds their rightful place and where every voice matters. title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong>About SecurityScorecard:</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">SecurityScorecard is the global leader in cybersecurity ratings, with over 12 million companies continuously rated, operating in 64 countries. Founded in 2013 by security and risk experts Dr. Alex Yampolskiy and Sam Kassoumeh and funded by world-class investors, SecurityScorecard’s patented rating technology is used by over 25,000 organizations for self-monitoring, third-party risk management, board reporting, and cyber insurance underwriting; making all organizations more resilient by allowing them to easily find and fix cybersecurity risks across their digital footprint.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Headquartered in New York City, our culture has been recognized by Inc Magazine as a "Best Workplace,” by Crain’s NY as a "Best Places to Work in NYC," and as one of the 10 hottest SaaS startups in New York for two years in a row. Most recently, SecurityScorecard was named to Fast Company’s annual list of the</span><a href="https://securityscorecard.com/blog/blog-top-innovators-of-the-year/"><span style="font-weight: 400;"> </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">World’s Most Innovative Companies for 2023</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> and to the Achievers 50 Most Engaged Workplaces in 2023 award recognizing “forward-thinking employers for their unwavering commitment to employee engagement.”&nbsp; SecurityScorecard is proud to be funded by world-class investors including Silver Lake Waterman, Moody’s, Sequoia Capital, GV and Riverwood Capital.</span></p> <p><strong>About the Team:<br><br></strong>Be part of an exciting and growing Technology team focused on product development. Our team emphasis is collaboration and shared responsibilities, you will be hands-on involved in the entire Software Development Life Cycle. Our team is growing and so will you! Check out our tech blog below and let us know if you are our next star.<br><br><a href="https://techblog.securityscorecard.com/">https://techblog.securityscorecard.com/</a></p> <p><strong><br>About the Role:<br></strong></p> <p><strong>What you will do</strong></p> <p>We are looking for a Senior Data Engineer who thrives on building commercial products and plays a key role in delivering reliable, scalable, and maintainable applications. You will own projects end-to-end, collaborating with Product Managers to define requirements, implement solutions, and ensure ongoing success post-deployment. You'll focus on simplicity while contributing technical insights to enhance the system's performance, stability, and user experience.</p> <ul> <li>Participate in all stages of the software development life cycle, from design and development to deployment and maintenance.</li> <li>Contribute to the product and technical roadmap by providing data-driven insights and articulating trade-offs between technical options and business goals.</li> <li>Support and mentor junior engineers on the team through code reviews, pairing, and technical guidance.</li> <li>Follow clean code principles, using best practices to write secure, testable, and well-documented software.</li> <li>Work closely with a global team, including engineers and product managers, to build a stable, high-performance platform.</li> <li>Implement comprehensive testing and performance tuning to ensure system stability and uptime.</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Translate requirements, estimate effort, and address or escalate blockers as needed.</li> <li>Write clean, maintainable code that adheres to best practices in readability, design patterns, reusability, and testing.</li> <li>Own end-to-end projects, understanding and contributing to all aspects (infrastructure, application tiers, and data tiers).</li> <li>Continuously monitor performance metrics and recommend improvements or refactors.</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Our Tech Stack:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Scala, Apache Spark, Python</li> <li>Airflow</li> <li>ScyllaDB, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Hive, Redis, Kafka, Kafka Connect</li> <li>Docker, Jenkins, Terraform, AWS</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Required Qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience working in the Data Engineering space, and with building and maintaining big data pipelines&nbsp;</li> <li>3+ years of experience working in agile environments (ideally Scrum), collaborating with cross-functional teams (engineering, design, product).</li> <li>Proficient in Spark</li> <li>Experience with strongly-typed languages (Java / Scala preferred)</li> <li>Experience designing, building, and maintaining RESTful APIs and integrating with external services.</li> <li>Participate in code reviews to ensure best practices, maintainability, and continuous improvement of the codebase.</li> <li>Ability to write and maintain unit and integration tests based on acceptance criteria, ensuring code quality and reliability.</li> <li>Proficiency with version control tools, particularly Git, for collaborative development and code management.</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Preferred Qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Strong understanding of building scalable and high-performance back-end systems, optimizing for low-latency and high-throughput.</li> <li>Worked with a variety of data (structured/unstructured), data formats (flat files, XML, JSON, relational, parquet).</li> <li>Experience in functional programming languages (Scala preferred)</li> <li>Experience in Cyber Security</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Communication and Collaboration:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Strong attention to documentation and maintaining standards across projects.</li> <li>Ability to present and defend technical decisions with confidence.</li> <li>Skilled at estimating project complexity based on early documentation and discussions.</li> <li>Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate technical challenges and solutions clearly.</li> </ul> <p><span data-sheets-value="{&quot;1&quot;:2,&quot;2&quot;:&quot;SecurityScorecard is an industry-leading cybersecurity company backed by Google, Sequoia, and Riverwood. Our mission is to make the world a safer place. We have created a new category of enterprise software, and our culture has helped us be recognized as one of the 10 hottest SaaS startups in NY for two years in a row.\nOur vision is to create a new language for companies and their partners to communicate, understand, and improve each other’s security posture.&quot;}" data-sheets-userformat="{&quot;2&quot;:15235,&quot;3&quot;:{&quot;1&quot;:0},&quot;4&quot;:[null,2,16043212],&quot;10&quot;:2,&quot;11&quot;:4,&quot;12&quot;:0,&quot;14&quot;:[null,2,0],&quot;15&quot;:&quot;\&quot;Proxima Nova\&quot;&quot;,&quot;16&quot;:10}"><strong><br>Benefits:</strong><br><br>Specific to each country, we offer a competitive salary, stock options, Health benefits, and unlimited PTO, parental leave, tuition reimbursements, and much more!</span></p> <p>The estimated total compensation range for this position is $75,000 - $92,000 (base plus bonus). Actual compensation for the position is based on a variety of factors, including, but not limited to affordability, skills, qualifications and experience, and may vary from the range. In addition to base salary, employees may also be eligible for annual performance-based incentive compensation awards and equity, among other company benefits.&nbsp;</p> <p><em><span style="font-weight: 400;">SecurityScorecard is committed to Equal Employment Opportunity and embraces diversity. full_description: <p><strong>About SecurityScorecard:</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">SecurityScorecard is the global leader in cybersecurity ratings, with over 12 million companies continuously rated, operating in 64 countries. Most recently, SecurityScorecard was named to Fast Company’s annual list of the</span><a href="https://securityscorecard.com/blog/blog-top-innovators-of-the-year/"><span style="font-weight: 400;"> </span><span style="font-weight: 400;">World’s Most Innovative Companies for 2023</span></a><span style="font-weight: 400;"> and to the Achievers 50 Most Engaged Workplaces in 2023 award recognizing “forward-thinking employers for their unwavering commitment to employee engagement.”&nbsp; SecurityScorecard is proud to be funded by world-class investors including Silver Lake Waterman, Moody’s, Sequoia Capital, GV and Riverwood Capital.</span></p> <p><strong>About the Team:<br><br></strong>Be part of an exciting and growing Technology team focused on product development. Our team emphasis is collaboration and shared responsibilities, you will be hands-on involved in the entire Software Development Life Cycle. Our team is growing and so will you! Check out our tech blog below and let us know if you are our next star.<br><br><a href="https://techblog.securityscorecard.com/">https://techblog.securityscorecard.com/</a></p> <p><strong><br>About the Role:<br></strong></p> <p><strong>What you will do</strong></p> <p>We are looking for a Senior Data Engineer who thrives on building commercial products and plays a key role in delivering reliable, scalable, and maintainable applications. You will own projects end-to-end, collaborating with Product Managers to define requirements, implement solutions, and ensure ongoing success post-deployment. You'll focus on simplicity while contributing technical insights to enhance the system's performance, stability, and user experience.</p> <ul> <li>Participate in all stages of the software development life cycle, from design and development to deployment and maintenance.</li> <li>Contribute to the product and technical roadmap by providing data-driven insights and articulating trade-offs between technical options and business goals.</li> <li>Support and mentor junior engineers on the team through code reviews, pairing, and technical guidance.</li> <li>Follow clean code principles, using best practices to write secure, testable, and well-documented software.</li> <li>Work closely with a global team, including engineers and product managers, to build a stable, high-performance platform.</li> <li>Implement comprehensive testing and performance tuning to ensure system stability and uptime.</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Responsibilities:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Translate requirements, estimate effort, and address or escalate blockers as needed.</li> <li>Write clean, maintainable code that adheres to best practices in readability, design patterns, reusability, and testing.</li> <li>Own end-to-end projects, understanding and contributing to all aspects (infrastructure, application tiers, and data tiers).</li> <li>Continuously monitor performance metrics and recommend improvements or refactors.</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Our Tech Stack:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Scala, Apache Spark, Python</li> <li>Airflow</li> <li>ScyllaDB, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Hive, Redis, Kafka, Kafka Connect</li> <li>Docker, Jenkins, Terraform, AWS</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Required Qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience working in the Data Engineering space, and with building and maintaining big data pipelines&nbsp;</li> <li>3+ years of experience working in agile environments (ideally Scrum), collaborating with cross-functional teams (engineering, design, product).</li> <li>Proficient in Spark</li> <li>Experience with strongly-typed languages (Java / Scala preferred)</li> <li>Experience designing, building, and maintaining RESTful APIs and integrating with external services.</li> <li>Participate in code reviews to ensure best practices, maintainability, and continuous improvement of the codebase.</li> <li>Ability to write and maintain unit and integration tests based on acceptance criteria, ensuring code quality and reliability.</li> <li>Proficiency with version control tools, particularly Git, for collaborative development and code management.</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Preferred Qualifications:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Strong understanding of building scalable and high-performance back-end systems, optimizing for low-latency and high-throughput.</li> <li>Worked with a variety of data (structured/unstructured), data formats (flat files, XML, JSON, relational, parquet).</li> <li>Experience in functional programming languages (Scala preferred)</li> <li>Experience in Cyber Security</li> </ul> <p><strong><br>Communication and Collaboration:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Strong attention to documentation and maintaining standards across projects.</li> <li>Ability to present and defend technical decisions with confidence.</li> <li>Skilled at estimating project complexity based on early documentation and discussions.</li> <li>Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate technical challenges and solutions clearly.</li> </ul> <p><span title: Data Engineering Manager
full_description: <p>Doximity is transforming the healthcare industry. Join our mission to help every physician be more productive and provide better care for their patients. As medicine's largest network in the United States, there's an elevated level of responsibility in everything we do. We don't take that responsibility lightly and are committed to building diverse teams with an inclusive culture that can make a direct impact on the healthcare system.</p> <p>One of Doximity's<a href="https://work.doximity.com/"> core values</a> is stretching ourselves. Even if you don't check off all the boxes below we encourage you to apply. Doximity is full of exceptional people who bring their own unique experiences to work everyday and make us all better for it!</p> <p><em>This role can be filled in our San Francisco headquarters OR remotely in the US or Canada.</em></p> <p><strong><em>About you</em></strong></p> <ul> <li><strong><em>Foremost an engineer</em></strong><em>.</em> You exemplify high code quality and guide others, and have at least <strong>5 years</strong> of industry experience as a data engineer or similar role.</li> <ul> <li>You are experienced in creating automated testing, applying design patterns, and other engineering best practices.</li> <li>You are an expert in Python and possess fluency in SQL and have developed maintainable data pipelines with each.</li> </ul> <li><strong><em>Experienced People Manager.</em></strong> You have experience managing 3 or more people whereby you were accountable for their growth and success as individuals and team members.</li> <li><strong><em>Communication Extraordinaire.</em></strong> You excel at communicating to both technical and non-technical people, with an ability to explain complex concepts simply.</li> <li><strong><em>Player Coach</em></strong><em>.</em> You are able to contribute individually, outside of your reports or team, balancing priorities between being a player (i.e. individual contributor) and a coach (i.e. manager or mentor).</li> <li><strong><em>See a project from end-to-end</em></strong><em>.</em> You have led several projects, your own ideas and other's. This means from idea generation to planning, execution, through delivering.</li> <li><strong><em>ML/AI–aware. </em></strong>You have working knowledge of how ML/AI applications are deployed and monitored, and you’re motivated to use this awareness to advance data-driven initiatives.</li> </ul> <p><strong><em>Here's How You Will Make an Impact</em></strong></p> <ul> <li><em>Collaborate with product managers, data analysts, and machine learning engineers to develop pipelines and ETL tasks in order to facilitate the extraction of insights.</em></li> <li><em>Establish data architecture processes and practices that can be scheduled, automated, replicated and serve as standards for other teams to leverage.</em><em>Inform data team strategy by working with the product leaders and managers. </em></li> <li><em>Actively participate in execution and some planning of organizational data team strategy. </em></li> <li><em>Collaborate with Doximity’s Data Leadership team to ensure alignment and success across the entire Data Organization.</em></li> <li><em>Manage individual Data Engineers to foster learning, growth and success at Doximity.</em></li> </ul> <p><strong><em>About Us</em></strong></p> <ul> <li><em><a href="https://technology.doximity.com/technology-stack">Explore our stack</a></em></li> <li><em>We have over 500 private repositories in Github containing our pipelines, our own internal multi-functional tools, and<a href="https://github.com/doximity"> open-source projects</a></em></li> <li><em>We have worked as a distributed team for a long time;<a href="https://blog.brunomiranda.com/building-a-distributed-engineering-team-85d281b9b1c"> we're currently about 65% distributed</a></em></li> <li><em>Find out more information </em><em>on the<a href="https://engineering.doximity.com/"> Doximity engineering blog</a></em></li> <li><em>Our<a href="https://work.doximity.com/"> company core values</a></em></li> <li><em>Our<a href="https://engineering.doximity.com/articles/engineering-recruitment-process-doximity"> recruiting process</a></em></li> <li><em>Our<a href="https://engineering.doximity.com/articles/mofo-driven-product-development"> product development cycle</a></em></li> <li><em>Our<a href="https://engineering.doximity.com/articles/software-engineering-on-boarding-at-doximity"> on-boarding &amp; mentorship process</a></em></li> </ul> <p><strong><em>Compensation</em></strong></p> <p><em>The US total compensation range for this full-time position is $172,000 - $254,000 (i</em><em>nclusive of salary + equity). Our ranges are determined by role and level. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the approximate total target compensation for the position across the US. Within the range, individual pay is determined by factors including relevant skills, experience, and education/training. Please note that the compensation listed does not include benefits.</em></p> <p><strong><em>More on /Benefits/Perks&nbsp;</em></strong></p> <p><em>Doximity is proud to offer industry-leading benefits to our full time employees. Some of our offerings include:&nbsp;</em></p> <ul> <li><em>Medical, dental, vision offerings for you and your family</em></li> <li><em>4</em><em>01k with matching program</em></li> <li><em>Employee stock purchase plan</em></li> <li><em>Family planning support, Childcare FSA, and parental leave</em></li> <li><em>Life, AD&amp;D, and Disability</em></li> <li><em>Generous time off, holidays and paid company trips</em></li> <li><em>Wellness benefits…plus many more!</em></li> </ul> <p><strong><em>More About Doximity…</em></strong></p> <p><em>For the past decade, it’s been our mission to help every physician be more productive so they can provide better care for their patients. We believe that when doctors are connected, the healthcare system works better and patients benefit. Doximity enables our verified clinician members to collaborate with colleagues, stay up-to-date with the latest medical news and research, manage their careers, and conduct virtual patient visits. Today, Doximity is the leading digital platform for U.S. medical professionals, with over 85% of physicians, 50% of all nurse practitioners and physician assistants, and 90% of graduating medical students as members.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><em>Joining Doximity means being part of an incredibly talented and humble team passionate about improving inefficiencies in our $4.3 trillion U.S. healthcare system. We are a team of doers who solve problems everyday by treating obstacles like an adventure, and we love creating technology that has a real, meaningful impact on people’s lives. Doxers are committed to working towards a more equitable world both within and beyond our office walls. This starts by fostering an inclusive and diverse work environment where differences are valued and all employees are encouraged to bring their full, authentic selves to work daily. To learn more about our team, culture, and users, check out our</em><a href="https://workat.doximity.com/"><em> </em><em>careers page</em></a><em>,</em><a href="https://blog.doximity.com/"><em> </em><em>company blog</em></a><em>, and</em><a href="https://engineering.doximity.com/"><em> </em><em>engineering blog</em></a><em>. We’re growing fast, and there’s plenty of opportunity for you to make an impact—join us! For more information, visit</em><a href="http://www.doximity.com/"><em> Doximity.com</em></a><em>.</em></p> <p><em>____________________________________________</em></p> <p><strong><em>EEOC Statement</em></strong></p> <p><em>Doximity is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and committed to providing employment opportunities regardless of race, religious creed, color, national origin, ancestry, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, marital status, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, pregnancy, childbirth and breastfeeding, age, sexual orientation, military or veteran status, or any other protected classification. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p>

title: Data Engineering Manager
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>SmarterDx is transforming how health systems use clinical AI to capture the full value of patient care delivered. Built by physician-data scientists and trained on clinically-validated EHR data, our clinical AI platform interprets the nuances behind every patient story and makes clinically-sound recommendations for revenue cycle teams — helping hospitals recover earned revenue, improve quality metrics, reduce denials, and streamline revenue cycle operations. As a Smartian, you’ll help build technology that makes healthcare more accurate, sustainable, and effective for everyone. Learn more at <span><a href="http://smarterdx.com/careers" target="_blank">smarterdx.com/careers</a></span>.</p></div><h3>Role</h3> <p>SmarterDx is seeking a Manager of Data Engineering, Integrations to lead and grow a high‑performing data engineering team responsible for ingesting, harmonizing, and operationalizing healthcare data (EHR, clinical, and claims) across our products. You will scale new‑client onboarding, raise the bar on data quality and governance, and ensure resilient, observable, and cost‑effective ELT pipelines. You will set clear objectives, mentor engineers, and partner closely with Product, Clinical, Data Science, and Engineering to deliver durable outcomes.</p> <p>SmarterDx builds clinical AI that empowers hospitals to analyze the complete record of every patient to fully capture the value of care delivered. Founded by physicians in 2020, its proprietary AI platform understands the nuances of clinical reasoning, enabling hospitals to true the patient record for every discharge. By doing so, hospitals can recover millions in earned revenue, enhance care quality metrics, and optimize healthcare operations. Become a Smartian and help optimize the way the healthcare system works for everyone. Learn more at smarterdx.com/careers.</p> <p><em>**This role is fully remote within the US**</em></p> <p><strong>What You'll Do</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead, coach, and develop a team of analytics engineers and/or data engineers with a focus on technical excellence, delivery velocity, and collaborative culture.</li> <li>Ensure on-time delivery of client data integrations by owning enterprise data model standards and maintaining consistent, governed data definitions.</li> <li>Oversee client data pipelines using modern tooling (dbt, Airflow, Snowflake, AWS, Python) to ensure reliable operation and uptime.</li> <li>Drive client integration delivery speed by implementing automation, reusable patterns, and clear runbooks to directly optimize client onboarding timelines.</li> <li>Establish and enforce SLOs/SLAs, instrument critical paths, and drive incident reduction via proactive monitoring, alerting, and post‑incident reviews.</li> <li>Implement comprehensive data quality and testing frameworks (schema, nullability, referential integrity, reconciliation) and lineage observability.</li> <li>Collaborate cross‑functionally (Product, Clinical, Data Science, Engineering) to prioritize roadmaps, land measurable outcomes, and manage tradeoffs.</li> <li>Manage high-volume client integration requests, triaging and prioritizing tickets based on client impact, urgency, and business value while maintaining clear communication on resolution timelines.</li> <li>Own daily operations rhythm including ticket review, resource allocation, escalation management, and stakeholder updates to ensure client integration commitments are met.</li> <li>Drive operational metrics and reporting on integration delivery performance, ticket resolution times, and team capacity to identify bottlenecks and improve client outcomes</li> </ul> <p><strong>What You Bring</strong></p> <ul> <li>7+ years of data engineering experience, including leadership of engineers and outcomes (hiring, mentoring, performance, delivery).</li> <li>Deep experience with healthcare datasets: clinical, and/or billing/claims; familiarity with HL7/FHIR and interoperability patterns.</li> <li>Strong SQL and data modeling expertise; proven track record building reliable ELT in dbt, Airflow, Snowflake, and Python.</li> <li>An Operational excellence mindset that integrates SLOs/SLAs, on‑call/incident management, root‑cause analysis, and preventive improvements.</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to accelerate client data onboarding through automation, standardization, and repeatable roll up sleeves and contribute hands‑on to unblock the team.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice To Haves</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience at a scale‑up or rapid‑growth technology company.</li> <li>Experience normalization.</li> <li>Certifications: Snowflake (SnowPro), AWS (Data Analytics or Solutions Architect), dbt, or healthcare data (CHDA/RHIA).</li> </ul> <p><strong>Our Tech Stack</strong></p> <ul> <li>Databases: Snowflake (primary), Postgres</li> <li>Cloud Infrastructure: AWS (CDK, S3, MWAA, EventBridge, Batch, Lambda, Step Functions), FiveTran</li> <li>Technologies: SQL, dbt, Python</li> </ul> title: Cyber Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong>**ACTIVE TS/SCI SECURITY CLEARANCE REQUIRED**</strong></p> <p>Build the infrastructure that keeps critical national security data flowing. You'll own the pipelines, systems, and integrations that power real cybersecurity investigations — and have the autonomy to solve complex engineering problems that actually matter.</p> <table style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 624px; border-style: solid;" border="1" width="624"> <tbody> <tr> <td width="104"> <p><strong>Linux / Windows</strong></p> </td> <td width="104"> <p><strong>SIEM (Splunk / Elastic)</strong></p> </td> <td width="104"> <p><strong>VMware / Hyper-V</strong></p> </td> <td width="104"> <p><strong>Networking &amp; Firewalls</strong></p> </td> <td width="104"> <p><strong>Python / Bash</strong></p> </td> <td width="104"> <p><strong>Cloud (AWS)</strong></p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>WHAT YOU'LL OWN</strong></p> <table style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 624px; border-width: 1px; border-style: inset;" border="1" width="624"> <tbody> <tr> <td style="border-width: 1px;" width="312"> <p><strong>Data Pipelines</strong></p> <p>Ingest, normalize, and manage event logs powering live security investigations.</p> </td> <td style="border-width: 1px;" width="312"> <p><strong>SIEM Management</strong></p> <p>Own Splunk, Elastic, or ArcSight deployments end-to-end — configs, patches, uptime.</p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td style="border-width: 1px;" width="312"> <p><strong>Host &amp; VM Administration</strong></p> <p>Linux and Windows systems, hypervisors (ESXi, Hyper-V), and cloud assets.</p> </td> <td style="border-width: 1px;" width="312"> <p><strong>Security Compliance</strong></p> <p>Apply federal, DoD, and IC configuration standards across all managed systems.</p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p><strong>WHAT YOU'LL NEED</strong></p> <ul> <li>5+ years in systems engineering or a similar infrastructure/ops role</li> <li>Advanced Linux proficiency — comfortable on the command line, can work through complex issues independently</li> <li>Strong networking fundamentals: DNS, DHCP, IPAM, load balancers, firewalls</li> <li>Hands-on hypervisor experience (VMware ESXi, Hyper-V, or Citrix XenServer)</li> <li>IAT II certification (or ability to obtain); CSSP Infrastructure Support cert required within 120 days of hire</li> <li>Proven root-cause analysis skills — you don't just fix symptoms, you find the source</li> <li>Excellent communication skills; comfortable working with both technical peers and non-technical stakeholders</li> </ul> <p><strong>BONUS POINTS</strong></p> <ul> <li>SIEM experience with Splunk, Elastic, or ArcSight</li> <li>Familiarity with Cribl for data aggregation and normalization</li> <li>Scripting in Python, Bash, or PowerShell</li> <li>Public cloud experience (AWS, GCP, Rackspace) or private cloud (VMware, OpenStack)</li> </ul> <p><strong>Additional Information</strong></p> <ul> <li>All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.</li> <li>Compensation is unique to each candidate and relative to the skills and experience they bring to the position. The salary range for this position is typically $140-$145k. This does not guarantee a specific salary as compensation is based upon multiple factors such as education, experience, certifications, and other requirements, and may fall outside of the above-stated range.</li> <li>Highlights of our benefits include Health/Dental/Vision, 401(k) match, Accrued PTO, STD/LTD/Life Insurance, Referral Bonuses, professional development reimbursement, and more!</li> </ul> <p><em>D2 Technical Services is committed to a merit-based recruitment process and encourages applications from all qualified individuals.&nbsp; As a Veteran-Owned Small Business, we particularly welcome applications from veterans who have the requisite skills and experience.&nbsp; Job applicants that are interested in one of our openings and may require a reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, should contact us to request an accommodation.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p>

title: Data Engineer
full_description: <p><strong><span data-olk-copy-source="MessageBody">What Impact You’ll Have</span></strong></p> <p>GRVTY’s team provides tactical data engineering solutions. We embed skilled Data&nbsp;Engineers, Data Scientists, and ETL Developers directly into intelligence analyst groups&nbsp;to be their go-to data wranglers. We develop new tools, code, and services to execute&nbsp;data engineering activities. Our engineers work to collect, process, and feed analytic&nbsp;tools, turning data into intelligence in response to immediate mission needs, with direct&nbsp;impact on real world situations. You will see your work used here on a daily basis, and&nbsp;you'll have the opportunity to support a variety of Sponsor mission organizations and&nbsp;mission partner organizations.<br>This is a time of development and growth on the program, with an increasing number of missions being supported. The work is high impact and important, and the customer moves quickly. The environment is fast-paced, flexible, and open to innovation - you'll have more latitude here in choosing how to achieve results than on many other projects. The customer cares more about what you can do as opposed to your years of<br>experience, and work hours are typically quite flexible - roll up your sleeves, get things&nbsp;done, and no one cares much about the specific hours that you work. The work space&nbsp;itself is also quite nice, and there is an excellent cafeteria!&nbsp;The tech stack on this team is rather huge and includes Python (Pandas, numpy, scipy,&nbsp;scikit-learn, standard libraries, etc.), Python packages that wrap Machine Learning&nbsp;(packages for NLP, Object Detection, etc.), Linux, AWS/C2S, Apache NiFi, Spark,&nbsp;pySpark, Hadoop, Kafka, ElasticSearch, Solr, Kibana, neo4J, MariaDB, Postgres,&nbsp;Docker, Puppet, and many others.&nbsp;Work on this program takes place in McLean, VA and in various field offices throughout&nbsp;Northern VA (we cannot support remote work) and requires a TS/SCI + Polygraph&nbsp;clearance (acceptable to this customer).</p> <p><strong><span data-olk-copy-source="MessageBody">What You’ll Be Owning</span></strong></p> <p>GRVTY is seeking a Data Engineer with a TS/SCI + Poly clearance (applicable to this customer) to join one of our top projects in Chantilly, VA.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>Responsibilities</p> <ul> <li>Develop new tools, code &amp; services to execute data engineering activities. Activities include the following tasks:&nbsp;</li> <li>Movement of structure &amp; unstructured data using approved methods. Execute data ingestion activities for storing data in a local or enterprise level location. View data in its source format.</li> <li>Develop code to format data that supports exploration. Analyze source data formats &amp; work with Data Scientists &amp; partners to determine the formats &amp; transforms that best meet mission objectives.</li> <li>Develop code and tools to provide one-time &amp; on-going data formatting &amp; transformations into enterprise or boutique data models. Implement existing ETL code &amp; best practices/standards.</li> <li>Develop an ETL Code Transition Plan. Develop &amp; deliver documentation for each project including ETL mappings, code use guide, code location &amp; access instructions.</li> <li>Facilitate Code Reviews. Provide consulting services to support data transport, ingestion, conditioning, access, &amp; management.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>What You Must Have</strong></p> <ul> <li>Active TS/SCI with Polygraph Clearance</li> <li>Ability and willingness to quickly learn a new tool&nbsp;</li> <li>Strong communication skills with both your teammates, and your leads&nbsp;</li> <li>SQL, Python, Pyspark experience.&nbsp;</li> <li>Willingness to do development type work when needed (junior level development at best)</li> <li>Ability to be a self starter and ask questions when needed&nbsp;</li> <li>Comfortable working with and manipulating data in compliance with the offices workflow</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Extract, Transform and Load (ETL) tools and processes.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li data-ogsc="black">AWS&nbsp;</li> <li data-ogsc="black">APIs&nbsp;</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Linux&nbsp;</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Geospatial tools/data<em data-ogsc="">&nbsp;</em></li> </ul> <p class="x_MsoNormal"><strong>What Would Be Nice to Have</strong></p> <ul type="disc"> <li data-ogsc="black" data-olk-copy-source="MessageBody">Palantir Foundry Experience</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Kubeflow&nbsp;</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Experience with OCR and text extraction of PDFs.&nbsp;</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Experience with data validation / data quality after ETLing to be sure it’s ready for end users</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Docker</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Jenkins</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Hadoop/Spark</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Kibana</li> <li data-ogsc="black">Kafka</li> <li data-ogsc="black">NiFi</li> <li data-ogsc="black">ElasticSearch&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p></p> <p><strong>Pay Range: </strong>At GRVTY, we understand that compensation is influenced by many factors—such as geographic location, federal contract labor categories, wage rates, prior experience, skillsets, education, and certifications.<br>We’re proud to offer a work environment that empowers our team to achieve a strong work-life balance. GRVTY provides competitive pay, comprehensive benefits, and meaningful opportunities for professional growth.<br>Our benefits package is designed to support the well-being of our employees and their families, and includes coverage in areas such as healthcare, financial wellness, retirement planning, family assistance, continued education, and paid time off.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p></p></div><div class="title">Virginia Pay Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$150,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$200,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p id="isPasted"><strong>Why Choose GRVTY</strong></p> <hr> <p>The toughest national security challenges demand vision and ingenuity, not just resources. We deliver mission and technical expertise to outpace our adversaries. We’re purpose-built to tackle the most entrenched, systemic national security issues around the world.</p> <p>We partner with our customers to help them overcome challenges in every corner of technology and defense—including the ones still being explored. Our growing capabilities create complementary advantages, giving on-the-ground operations the edge they need to succeed. We muster everything we have to answer every challenge presented, every day of our lives.</p> <p>At GRVTY, we believe that when our employees thrive, our company thrives. That’s why we offer a comprehensive and competitive benefits package designed to support your well-being, growth, and work-life balance.</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;Robust health plan including medical, dental, and vision &nbsp;</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;Health Savings Account with company contribution &nbsp;</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;Annual Paid Time Off and Paid Holidays &nbsp;</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;Paid Parental Leave &nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;401k with generous company match &nbsp;</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;Training and Development Opportunities &nbsp;</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;Award Programs &nbsp;</p> <p>• &nbsp; &nbsp;Variety of Company Sponsored Events</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>EEO Statement</strong></p> <hr> <p>GRVTY, is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability.&nbsp;</p> <p>Anyone requiring reasonable accommodations should email <a href="mailto:recruiting@roycegeo.com" target="_blank">recruiting@grvty.com</a> or call 703-544-7930 with requested details. A member of the HR team will respond to your request within 2 business days. </p> <p><a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2022-10/EEOC_KnowYourRights_screen_reader_10_20.pdf" target="_blank">Know Your Rights: Workplace Discrimination is Illegal (eeoc.gov)</a> &nbsp;</p> <p>Please review our current job openings and apply for the positions you believe may be a fit. If you are not an immediate fit, we will also keep your resume in our database for future opportunities.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p></div>

title: 2026 - Internship, Data Engineering
full_description: <p><strong><span data-contrast="none">Programme duration:</span></strong><span data-contrast="none"> from 5 to 6 months, starting in 2026. </span><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;201341983&quot;:0,&quot;335551550&quot;:6,&quot;335551620&quot;:6,&quot;335559739&quot;:160,&quot;335559740&quot;:259}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p><strong><span data-contrast="none">Who qualifies:</span></strong><span data-contrast="none"><span class="TextRun SCXW215040191 BCX8" lang="EN-GB" data-contrast="auto"><span class="NormalTextRun SCXW215040191 BCX8"> Penultimate or final year students completing a Bachelor's, Master's.</span></span></span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong><span data-contrast="none">Qube Research &amp; Technologies (QRT)</span></strong><span data-contrast="none"> is a global quantitative and systematic investment manager, operating in all liquid asset classes across the world. We are a technology and data driven group implementing a scientific approach to investing. Combining data, research, technology, and trading expertise has shaped our collaborative mindset which enables us to solve the most complex challenges. QRT’s culture of innovation continuously drives our ambition to deliver high quality returns for our investors. </span><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span data-contrast="none">Over the years, QRT has invested in a global research and execution platform which has been deployed to cover all geographies and asset classes. This platform covers a broad spectrum from high to low frequency trading systems. We thrive at the intersection of cutting-edge technology, smart automation, and scalable processes, enabling us to move fast, think big, and deliver at scale. </span><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span data-contrast="none">We are committed to identifying and developing exceptional talent, and are inviting a new cohort of outstanding individuals to join us in the year ahead.&nbsp;Our internship offers a stimulating, intellectually rigorous, and high-performance environment, where collaboration is key to success.&nbsp;You will work alongside and be mentored by industry-leading professionals, gaining invaluable experience and positioning yourself for the opportunity to secure a full-time graduate role upon successful completion of the program.</span><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span data-contrast="none"> </span><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p><strong><span data-contrast="none">Your future role at QRT</span></strong><span data-contrast="none"> </span><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span data-contrast="none">Throughout the recruitment process, we will work to align your skills, interests, and potential with the teams where you can make the greatest impact. As part of QRT’s data teams, you will work at the heart of our research and trading operations - designing, building, and managing the data solutions that underpin our strategies. You will collaborate closely with Traders, Researchers, and Engineers to ensure our data infrastructure is robust, scalable, and able to power cutting-edge quantitative research. Your work will involve tackling complex challenges in data acquisition, engineering, and platform design.</span><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span data-contrast="none">Examples of potential roles and projects include:</span><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li data-leveltext="" data-font="Symbol" data-listid="1" data-list-defn-props="{&quot;335551671&quot;:0,&quot;335552541&quot;:1,&quot;335559685&quot;:720,&quot;335559991&quot;:360,&quot;469769226&quot;:&quot;Symbol&quot;,&quot;469769242&quot;:[8226],&quot;469777803&quot;:&quot;left&quot;,&quot;469777804&quot;:&quot;&quot;,&quot;469777815&quot;:&quot;multilevel&quot;}" data-aria-posinset="0" data-aria-level="1"><strong><span data-contrast="none">Quantitative Data Analyst</span></strong><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></li> </ul> <p><span data-contrast="none">Source, design, and onboard new datasets in line with priorities set by trading desks. Support data integration into trading systems, propose data-driven solutions to unlock new research opportunities, and manage the full lifecycle of data sourcing projects, from acquisition and exploration to design. Collaborate with Data Engineers to bring datasets into production at scale.</span><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;335559685&quot;:0}">&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li data-leveltext="" data-font="Symbol" data-listid="2" data-list-defn-props="{&quot;335551671&quot;:0,&quot;335552541&quot;:1,&quot;335559685&quot;:720,&quot;335559991&quot;:360,&quot;469769226&quot;:&quot;Symbol&quot;,&quot;469769242&quot;:[8226],&quot;469777803&quot;:&quot;left&quot;,&quot;469777804&quot;:&quot;&quot;,&quot;469777815&quot;:&quot;multilevel&quot;}" data-aria-posinset="0" data-aria-level="1"><strong><span data-contrast="none">Data Engineer</span></strong><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></li> </ul> <p><span data-contrast="none">Manage extensive datasets across QRT’s research and trading platforms. Design and implement data stores and APIs, monitor the health and performance of data processes, and deliver production-ready datasets. Provide direct support to Researchers and Traders to ensure they can fully leverage the capabilities of our data platform.</span><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;335559685&quot;:0}">&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li data-leveltext="" data-font="Symbol" data-listid="3" data-list-defn-props="{&quot;335551671&quot;:0,&quot;335552541&quot;:1,&quot;335559685&quot;:720,&quot;335559991&quot;:360,&quot;469769226&quot;:&quot;Symbol&quot;,&quot;469769242&quot;:[8226],&quot;469777803&quot;:&quot;left&quot;,&quot;469777804&quot;:&quot;&quot;,&quot;469777815&quot;:&quot;multilevel&quot;}" data-aria-posinset="0" data-aria-level="1"><strong><span data-contrast="none">Data Platform Engineer</span></strong><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></li> </ul> <p><span data-contrast="none">Build and maintain the large-scale data infrastructure that powers our research environment. Focus on the ingestion, processing, and serving of data for both real-time and batch workflows, ensuring the platform operates with low latency and high availability. Continuously monitor and optimize system performance to handle growing volumes of complex datasets efficiently.</span><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;335559685&quot;:0}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span title: Senior Data Engineer
full_description: <p>Qube Research &amp; Technologies (QRT) is a global quantitative and systematic investment manager, operating in all liquid asset classes across the world. We are a technology and data driven group implementing a scientific approach to investing. Combining data, research, technology and trading expertise has shaped QRT’s collaborative mindset which enables us to solve the most complex challenges. QRT’s culture of innovation continuously drives our ambition to deliver high quality returns for our investors.</p> <p class="PDq2pG_selectionAnchorContainer" data-section-id="1vkr844" data-start="131" data-end="162"><strong>Your future role within QRT:</strong></p> <ul data-start="164" data-end="1054"> <li data-section-id="1u4oq" data-start="164" data-end="258">Design, build, and operate the data platform powering quantitative research and backtesting.</li> <li data-section-id="njcskr" data-start="259" data-end="378">Develop production-grade Python applications to acquire, process, validate, and distribute market and reference data.</li> <li data-section-id="1d05pte" data-start="379" data-end="534">Build and operate scalable, automated data pipelines using technologies such as Apache Airflow and AWS Batch across AWS and QRT's compute infrastructure.</li> <li data-section-id="ubjxqp" data-start="535" data-end="641">Ensure data quality, validation, reconciliation, and observability across critical production pipelines.</li> <li data-section-id="3i7as1" data-start="642" data-end="759">Design systems that efficiently process large-scale datasets while supporting compute-intensive research workloads.</li> <li data-section-id="1bc3udz" data-start="760" data-end="925">Collaborate closely with quantitative researchers, software engineers, and infrastructure teams to accelerate dataset onboarding and improve research productivity.</li> <li data-section-id="jlo1vx" data-start="926" data-end="1054">Drive engineering best practices with a strong focus on software quality, automation, scalability, and operational excellence.</li> </ul> <p data-section-id="fg3ua7" data-start="1056" data-end="1081"><strong>Your present skillset:</strong></p> <ul data-start="1083" data-end="1790"> <li data-section-id="zem1wa" data-start="1083" data-end="1188">5+ years of experience building production-grade data platforms or large-scale data processing systems.</li> <li data-section-id="11kld55" data-start="1189" data-end="1237">Strong Python software engineering experience.</li> <li data-section-id="oq1k94" data-start="1238" data-end="1324">Hands-on experience with Apache Airflow or similar workflow orchestration platforms.</li> <li data-section-id="1ewilk1" data-start="1325" data-end="1418">Experience with AWS services including S3, EC2, AWS Batch, IAM, CloudWatch, and cloud SDKs.</li> <li data-section-id="b85nuq" data-start="1419" data-end="1510">Strong understanding of data quality, validation, testing, monitoring, and observability.</li> <li data-section-id="boh0b7" data-start="1511" data-end="1572">Good understanding of distributed data processing concepts.</li> <li data-section-id="uysfv8" data-start="1573" data-end="1655">Strong Linux skills and experience developing and supporting production systems.</li> <li data-section-id="c0dh" data-start="1656" data-end="1790">Excellent problem-solving skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across research, engineering, and infrastructure teams.</li> </ul> <p data-section-id="gx325i" data-start="1792" data-end="1808"><strong>Nice to have:</strong></p> <ul data-start="1810" data-end="2208"> <li data-section-id="dl3xvf" data-start="1810" data-end="1861">Experience working with market or financial data.</li> <li data-section-id="1viufk5" data-start="1862" data-end="1948">Knowledge of non-equity asset classes (e.g. futures, fixed income, commodities, FX).</li> <li data-section-id="17x5ppk" data-start="1949" data-end="1978">C++ development experience.</li> <li data-section-id="mtszw1" data-start="1979" data-end="1997">SQL proficiency.</li> <li data-section-id="fcjxi7" data-start="1998" data-end="2093">Experience with Docker, Kubernetes, and Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform or CloudFormation).</li> <li data-section-id="1sdrvxa" data-start="2094" data-end="2208">Familiarity with Apache Parquet, Apache Arrow, or distributed processing frameworks such as Spark, Ray, or Dask.</li> </ul> <p>QRT is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome diversity as essential to our success. QRT empowers employees to work openly and respectfully to achieve collective success. In addition to professional achievement, we are offering initiatives and programs to enable employees achieve a healthy work-life balance.</p> <div> <div class="flex h-svh w-screen flex-col"> <div class="relative z-0 flex min-h-0 w-full flex-1"> <div class="relative flex min-h-0 w-full min-w-0 flex-1"> <div class="group/side-pane-shell-host relative flex min-h-0 min-w-0 flex-1" data-side-pane-shell-host="true"> <div class="@container/main relative flex min-w-0 flex-1 flex-col -translate-y-[calc(env(safe-area-inset-bottom,0px)/2)] pt-[calc(env(safe-area-inset-bottom,0px)/2)]">&nbsp;</div> </div> </div> </div> </div> </div>

title: Treasury Data Engineer
full_description: <p>Qube Research &amp; Technologies (QRT) is a global quantitative and systematic investment manager, operating in all liquid asset classes across the world. We are a technology and data driven group implementing a scientific approach to investing. Combining data, research, technology, and trading expertise has shaped QRT's collaborative mindset which enables us to solve the most complex challenges. QRT's culture of innovation continuously drives our ambition to deliver high quality returns for our investors.</p> <p>The Treasury function is the financing backbone of QRT, managing the firm’s funding, collateral, margin, cash balances, and counterparty relationships across geographies and asset classes. The Treasury Analytics team builds the data infrastructure and tooling that gives the desk visibility into financing costs, margin requirements, and cash movements. The systems we build direct inform how the firm deploys its capital.</p> <p>Join our Treasury Analytics team in London as a Data Engineer, supporting and evolving the data infrastructure that powers Treasury's analytics platform, enabling the desk to analyse and optimise how the firm finances its positions in real time.</p> <p><strong>Role responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Design, build and maintain data pipelines to ingest, transform, and serve Treasury data across multiple sources and analytics systems</li> <li>Develop and maintain analytical tools to optimise margin, collateral, cash balances, and financing positions across counterparties</li> <li>Build the datasets and APIs that power Treasury's analytics platform, enabling the desk to optimise financing costs, monitor counterparty exposure, and manage liquidity in real time</li> <li>Evolve the infrastructure as the desk grows by onboarding new data sources, adapting to new requirements, and improving performance and reliability over time</li> <li>Onboard new storage and modelling layers, leveraging the right frameworks for different workloads</li> <li>Collaborate with desk leadership, traders, and engineers to implement robust, maintainable solutions</li> </ul> <p><strong>Required experience and skills</strong></p> <ul> <li>3–6 years' experience building high-performance intraday data applications, ideally in financial services</li> <li>Strong SQL, Python, and data modelling skills</li> <li>Understanding of data storage formats and trade-offs</li> <li>Experience with query optimisation and performance tuning</li> <li>Experience with modern orchestration frameworks (e.g. Airflow), cloud platforms, backend web services and networking protocols</li> <li>Knowledge of streaming systems and real-time data applications a plus</li> <li>Strong technical communication skills and ability to work effectively with a variety of stakeholders</li> </ul> <p><strong>Location</strong></p> <p>London</p>

title: Forward Deployed Product Manager – AI
location: Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States, New York, New York, United States
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">Iterative Health is a healthcare technology and services company powering the acceleration of clinical research to transform patient outcomes.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt;">We built a leading performance-driven network of 100+ sites across the US, Europe, India, and Australia, conducting research directly in the communities where care is delivered across gastrointestinal, hepatology, obesity, and cardiology. By combining deep clinical trial expertise with cutting-edge AI, we connect sponsors' scientific ambitions with high-performing research teams that expedite and expand access to novel therapeutics for patients in need. Today, Iterative Health is headquartered in Cambridge, Massachusetts, and New York City with 250+ employees world-wide.</span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p></div><p><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">At<a href="https://iterative.health/?utm_source=chatgpt.com"> Iterative Health</a>, we are transforming clinical research through the power of AI, technology, and human expertise. Iterative Health is a healthcare technology and services company powering the acceleration of clinical research to improve patient outcomes. Through the Iterative Health Site Network — a premier network of 100+ clinical research sites across the US and Europe — we help expand and expedite access to novel therapeutics for patients in need.</span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Accelerating clinical research is one of the defining challenges in healthcare. Promising therapies exist that patients can't access because the operational infrastructure to run clinical trials efficiently doesn't exist yet. We're building it.</span></p> <h2><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>Why This Role Matters</strong></span></h2> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">As a Forward Deployed AI Product Manager at Iterative Health, you will work cross functionally to identify, build, and deploy AI-powered solutions that transform how clinical research operations are executed. </span><span style="font-size: 10pt;">This is a highly hands-on role that sits at the intersection of product, engineering, and operations. You will partner closely with end users to understand their workflows, identify opportunities where AI can create meaningful leverage, rapidly prototype solutions, and deploy them into production. </span><span style="font-size: 10pt;">The ideal candidate combines strong product instincts with technical fluency and a builder mentality. You are equally comfortable sitting alongside a clinical operator to understand a workflow as you are working with engineers and AI tooling to bring a solution to life.</span></span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;">You will work across Clinical Operations, Site Operations, Trial Enrollment, Business Development, and Corporate Functions, identifying high-impact opportunities and owning them from concept through deployment and adoption. </span><span style="font-size: 10pt;">This role reports to the Product organization and partners closely with Engineering, Data Science, Security, and Operational Leadership teams.</span></span></p> <p><span style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>Where You’ll Drive Impact&nbsp;</strong></span></p> <ul> <li style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;
