Agility, passion and teamwork set us up to do our best work and foster a culture where you are empowered to craft your career, make an impact, and own (y)our future. Unleash the power of you!</p> <p><strong>About the role:</strong></p> <p>We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Big Data Engineer to join our team. The ideal candidate must have <span style="text-decoration: underline;"><strong>a minimum of 4 years of experience</strong></span> managing data engineering jobs in big data environment e.g., Cloudera Data Platform. Overall experience can be <span style="text-decoration: underline;"><strong>4 to 10 years</strong></span>. Consultant/ Senior Consultant preferably. The successful candidate will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining the data ingestion and processing jobs as per business requirements. Candidate will also be integrating data sets to provide seamless data access to users.</p> <p><strong>Key responsibilities:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Analyze the Authority’s data needs and document the requirements.</li> <li>Refine data collection/consumption by migrating data collection to more efficient channels.</li> <li>Plan, design and implement data engineering jobs and reporting solutions to meet the analytical needs.</li> <li>Develop test plan and scripts for system testing, support user acceptance testing.</li> <li>Work with the Authority’s technical teams to ensure smooth deployment and adoption of new solution.</li> <li>Ensure the smooth operations and service level of IT solutions.</li> <li>Support production issues - Track record in implementing systems with high availability, high performance, high security.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>The ideal candidate has:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Good understanding and completion of projects using waterfall/Agile methodology.</li> <li>Good understanding of analytics and <span style="text-decoration: underline;"><strong>data warehouse</strong></span> implementations.</li> <li>Ability to troubleshoot complex issues ranging from system resource to application stack traces.</li> <li>Strong SQL, data modelling and data analysis skills are a must.</li> <li>Hands-on experience in big data engineering jobs using <span style="text-decoration: underline;"><strong>Python, PySpark, Linux, and ETL tools like Informatica</strong></span>.</li> <li>Track record in implementing systems using Hive, Impala and Cloudera Data Platform will be preferred.</li> <li>Hands-on experience in DevOps deployment and data virtualization tools like Denodo will be preferred.</li> <li>Understanding/ Hands on experience of reporting or visualization tool like <span style="text-decoration: underline;"><strong>SAP BO and Tableau</strong></span> will be beneficial but not required.</li> <li>Passion for automation, standardization, and best practices.&nbsp;</li> <li>Good written and verbal communication and interpersonal skills, ability to understand the business requirement, communicate confidently with stakeholders.<strong>&nbsp; &nbsp;</strong></li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>AvePoint is proud to employ talent from many different backgrounds, experiences, and identities. We believe that diversity and inclusion drives our success and is at the core of how we hire, communicate, and collaborate to deliver value and excellence. We are committed to fostering an environment where people can bring their whole selves to work and feel a sense of belonging, and we continue to work toward creating a workforce that represents the diversity of our customers and communities. &nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><span data-teams="true"><span class="ui-provider a b c d e f g h i j k l m n o p q r s t u v w x y z ab ac ae af ag ah ai aj ak">Any personal data you share with us during the application process will be processed strictly in compliance with applicable data protection laws and our <a id="menur12n" class="fui-Link ___1q1shib f2hkw1w f3rmtva f1ewtqcl fyind8e f1k6fduh f1w7gpdv fk6fouc fjoy568 figsok6 f1s184ao f1mk8lai fnbmjn9 f1o700av f13mvf36 f1cmlufx f9n3di6 f1ids18y f1tx3yz7 f1deo86v f1eh06m1 f1iescvh fhgqx19 f1olyrje f1p93eir f1nev41a f1h8hb77 f1lqvz6u f10aw75t fsle3fq f17ae5zn" href="https://www.avepoint.com/company/privacy-notice" target="_blank">Privacy Notice</a>.</span></span></p></div>

title: Data Engineer
full_description: <p>.</p> <table width="682"> <tbody> <tr> <td width="682"> <p><strong>About Nscale</strong></p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td width="682"> <p>Nscale is the GPU cloud engineered for AI. We provide cost-effective, high-performance infrastructure for AI start-ups and large enterprise customers.&nbsp; Nscale enables AI-focused companies to achieve superior results by reducing the complexity of AI development. Our GPU cloud bolsters technical capabilities and directly supports strategic business outcomes, including cost management, rapid innovation, and environmental responsibility.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>We thrive on a culture of relentless innovation, ownership, and accountability, where every team member takes pride in their work and drives it with excellence and urgency. As an Nscaler, you’ll build trust through openness and transparency, where everyone is inspired to do their best work. If you join our team, you’ll be contributing to building the technology that powers the future.</p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <table width="682"> <tbody> <tr> <td width="682"> <p><strong>About the Role (Job Purpose)</strong></p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td width="682"> <p>We’re looking for a <strong>Data Engineer</strong> to help design, build, and operate the data foundations that underpin Nscale’s platform, internal operations, and customer-facing capabilities.</p> <p>This is a <strong>high-impact, early-stage role</strong>. You’ll work closely with Operations, Infrastructure, Platform Engineering, Product, and Commercial teams to turn raw operational signals — from GPUs, clusters, customers, and internal systems — into reliable, scalable data products that ensure delivery at a previously unseen pace. You’ll help define <em>how data is collected, modelled, served, and trusted</em> across the company, and have the opportunity to build on Palantir Foundry, as well as other products.</p> <p>This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building data systems from first principles, thrives in ambiguous environments, and wants to see their work directly influence product decisions, platform reliability, and customer outcomes.</p> </td> </tr> </tbody> </table> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <table width="681"> <tbody> <tr> <td width="681"> <p><strong>What You’ll be Doing (Responsibilities)</strong></p> </td> </tr> <tr> <td width="681"> <h4><strong>Data Platform &amp; Architecture</strong></h4> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Design and build scalable, reliable data pipelines that ingest data from infrastructure, platform services, and business systems.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Define data models and schemas that support operational workflows and use cases across the business, monitoring, and analytics.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Clean, transform and structure the data to create a digital twin of Nscale.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Implement permissioning and manage access and security of the Foundry implementation.<br><br></p> <h4><strong>Data Products &amp; Enablement</strong></h4> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Create trusted datasets and metrics that power workflows and processes, internal tools, and customer-facing insights.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Enable self-serve analytics by establishing clear data contracts, documentation, and semantic layers.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Build use cases including but not limited to capacity planning, cost optimisation, reliability analysis, and customer reporting to drive our business forward.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Collaborate with Product and Commercial teams to translate real-world questions into robust data solutions.<br><br></p> <h4><strong>Reliability, Quality &amp; Governance</strong></h4> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Implement data quality checks, monitoring, and alerting to ensure data correctness and availability.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Codify data lineage, freshness, and consistency across systems.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Establish best practices around data versioning, access control, and governance appropriate for a fast-scaling company.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Continuously improve system resilience and observability.<br><br></p> <h4><strong>Early-Stage Ownership &amp; Growth</strong></h4> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Take end-to-end ownership of projects, from design through to production and iteration.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Help define standards, tooling, and ways of working for data at Nscale.<br><br></p> <p>●&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; title: Data Engineer (Forward Deployed)
full_description: <p>&nbsp;</p> <div> <p><strong>About Nscale</strong></p> <p>Nscale is the GPU cloud engineered for AI. We provide cost-effective, high-performance infrastructure for AI start-ups and large enterprise customers.&nbsp; Nscale enables AI-focused companies to achieve superior results by reducing the complexity of AI development. Our GPU cloud bolsters technical capabilities and directly supports strategic business outcomes, including cost management, rapid innovation, and environmental responsibility.</p> <p>We thrive on a culture of relentless innovation, ownership, and accountability, where every team member takes pride in their work and drives it with excellence and urgency. As an Nscaler, you’ll build trust through openness and transparency, where everyone is inspired to do their best work. If you join our team, you’ll be contributing to building the technology that powers the future.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>About the Role</strong></p> <p>We’re looking for a Data Engineer to help design, build, and operate the data foundations that underpin Nscale’s platform, internal operations, and customer-facing capabilities.</p> <p>This is a high-impact, early-stage role. You’ll work closely with Operations, Infrastructure, Platform Engineering, Product, and Commercial teams to turn raw operational signals — from GPUs, clusters, customers, and internal systems — into reliable, scalable data products that ensure delivery at a previously unseen pace. You’ll help define&nbsp;<em>how data is collected, modelled, served, and trusted</em>&nbsp;across the company, and have the opportunity to build on Palantir Foundry, as well as other products.</p> <p>This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building data systems from first principles, thrives in ambiguous environments, and wants to see their work directly influence product decisions, platform reliability, and customer outcomes.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What you'll be doing</strong></p> <h4><strong>Data Platform &amp; Architecture</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Design and build scalable, reliable data pipelines that ingest data from infrastructure, platform services, and business systems.</li> <li>Define data models and schemas that support operational workflows and use cases across the business, monitoring, and analytics.</li> <li>Clean, transform and structure the data to create a digital twin of Nscale.</li> <li>Implement permissioning and manage access and security of the Foundry implementation.</li> </ul> <h4><strong>Data Products &amp; Enablement</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Create trusted datasets and metrics that power workflows and processes, internal tools, and customer-facing insights.</li> <li>Enable self-serve analytics by establishing clear data contracts, documentation, and semantic layers.</li> <li>Build use cases including but not limited to capacity planning, cost optimisation, reliability analysis, and customer reporting to drive our business forward.</li> <li>Collaborate with Product and Commercial teams to translate real-world questions into robust data solutions.</li> </ul> <h4><strong>Reliability, Quality &amp; Governance</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Implement data quality checks, monitoring, and alerting to ensure data correctness and availability.</li> <li>Codify data lineage, freshness, and consistency across systems.</li> <li>Establish best practices around data versioning, access control, and governance appropriate for a fast-scaling company.</li> <li>Continuously improve system resilience and observability.</li> </ul> <h4><strong>Early-Stage Ownership &amp; Growth</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Take end-to-end ownership of projects, from design through to production and iteration.</li> <li>Help define standards, tooling, and ways of working for data at Nscale.</li> <li>Contribute to technical decision-making as the company scales its platform and customer base.</li> <li>Act as a thought partner to engineers and operators, not just a service function.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>About You&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Deep, hands-on experience building in Palantir Foundry, including ontology modelling, pipeline development, API integration, and large-scale data platform design.</li> <li>Strong proficiency in Python, with experience applying data engineering libraries and frameworks (e.g. Spark, PySpark, Dask, pandas) to work with large, complex datasets.</li> <li>Familiarity with API-driven data integration, including REST, GraphQL, and Foundry Action APIs.</li> <li>Practical experience working in Git-based development workflows, including code reviews, version control, and CI/CD pipelines.</li> <li>Comfort working in ambiguous, early-stage environments where requirements evolve quickly.</li> <li>Strong communication skills — able to explain data concepts clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders.</li> <li>A bias toward ownership, pragmatism, and shipping useful solutions.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Nice to Have</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and infrastructure telemetry.</li> <li>Familiarity with distributed systems, monitoring data, or usage-based billing data.</li> <li>Experience supporting customer-facing data products or platforms.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What we can offer you&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>At Nscale, you'll find a collaborative, supportive, and innovative environment where your contributions spark real impact. We're building something extraordinary, and we want you at the core.</p> <ul> <li>Highly competitive package (base + equity) with reviews every 12 months. 🚀</li> <li>Join the fastest-growing tech startup, your chance to push boundaries, collaborate with brilliant minds, and make your mark on cutting-edge AI. ✨</li> <li>Expect a dynamic progression plan tailored to your ambitions. Grow by trying new things, leading, challenging the status quo, and owning your impact, always with our full support.</li> <li>Human-First Flexibility: We treat you as humans first. 🫶🏽 Our flexible workplace trusts Nscalers to deliver, giving you the autonomy to shape your day around life's moments.</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Equal Opportunities Statement</strong></p> <p>We strongly encourage applications from people of colour, the LGBTQ+ community, people with disabilities, neurodivergent people, parents, carers, and people from lower socio-economic backgrounds.</p> <p>If there’s anything we can do to accommodate your specific situation, please let us know.</p> <p>The responsibilities outlined in this job description are not exhaustive and are intended to provide a general overview of the position. The employee may be required to perform additional duties, tasks, and responsibilities as assigned by management, consistent with the skills and qualifications required for the role.</p> </div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><em>For information on how Nscale handles candidate personal data, please see our Employee &amp; Candidate Privacy Notice:&nbsp;<a href="https://drive.google.com/file/d/1QK5Yg04WHD9K9IAtJgQWubJZC9oLvatK/view?usp=sharing" target="_blank" data-saferedirecturl="https://www.google.com/url?q=https://drive.google.com/file/d/1QK5Yg04WHD9K9IAtJgQWubJZC9oLvatK/view?usp%3Dsharing&amp;source=gmail&amp;ust=1765375172804000&amp;usg=AOvVaw2Ncte4rmlGl8OKuFuDgDtx">Here.</a></em></p></div>

title: Google Cloud Platform Data Engineer
full_description: <div style="font-size: 10pt; font-family: 'Tahoma';"> <p>(ID: 2026-2740)</p> <br> <p><strong>Axle Informatics</strong> is a bioscience and information technology company advancing translational research through biomedical informatics, data science, artificial intelligence, and intelligent automation. We partner with research centers, healthcare organizations, and government agencies around the globe to transform complex data into actionable insights that accelerate scientific discovery and improve decision-making. Our multidisciplinary teams of experts in biomedical science, software engineering, data engineering, AI, automation, and program management develop innovative technologies, modernize data ecosystems, and create scalable solutions that enable the next generation of research and healthcare innovation.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <div class="ck-content" style="font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;" data-wrapper="true"> <div style="font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;"> <p style="border-style: none; margin: 15.0pt 0in 5.0pt; padding: 0in;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Role Overview</strong></span></p> </div> <p style="margin: 4.0pt 0in; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">We are seeking an experienced GCP Data Engineer to own the technical pipeline infrastructure for a Finance Transformation program. The successful candidate will design and implement the shared pipeline architecture that underpins the program's data product delivery, building the Workday and Oracle Finance connectors and owning the unified data model, lineage graph, and real time event capture infrastructure.</span></p> <p style="margin: 4.0pt 0in; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">This is a senior, hands on engineering role requiring deep expertise in GCP pipeline services, Workday and Oracle API extraction, and data lineage architecture. The candidate will establish the GCP native pipeline pattern used by both domain connectors, pair with each domain analyst on their source specific extraction builds, and ensure the unified data model in Cloud SQL serves all three reporting lenses -- Function, Supervisory Organization, and Job Family -- from a single join layer.</span></p> <p style="margin: 4.0pt 0in; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">The successful candidate will combine engineering rigor with practical delivery focus, able to move between architectural design and production build, and provide clear technical guidance to domain analysts who own the domain specific extraction logic.</span></p> <div style="font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;"> <p style="border-style: none; margin: 15.0pt 0in 5.0pt; padding: 0in;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;"><strong>Key Responsibilities</strong></span></p> </div> <ul> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="e108efed1e6cd9f4b39f449bf367e3bbc"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Design and implement the shared GCP native pipeline architecture, establishing the Cloud Dataflow and Cloud Composer pattern that both HR and Finance connectors follow, with pipeline health monitoring, alerting, and audit trail via Cloud Monitoring and Cloud Logging.</span></p> </li> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="e0207a20eaa6577fc9af70c192640d1d9"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Build the Workday connector using RaaS for bulk extraction and REST APIs for real time lifecycle event capture, and configure Firebase Realtime Database to reflect classification and cost changes within minutes of each lifecycle event.</span></p> </li> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="e36c8fd60a584b511088ee57728a0d731"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; text-align: start;"><span style="color: #000000; font-family: Tahoma; font-size: 10pt;">Build the Oracle Finance connector using REST APIs with SOAP fallback, applying basis tagging at ingestion so the same pipeline serves statutory, management, Lloyd's, and regulatory reporting without rerunning the extract.</span></p> </li> <li class="ck-list-marker-font-size ck-list-marker-color ck-list-marker-font-family" data-list-item-id="e20f778cae742f824c195f53f103090c2"> <p style="margin: 2.5pt 0in 2.5pt 0; title: Data Engineer (Starlink)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>SpaceX was founded under the belief that a future where humanity is out exploring the stars is fundamentally more exciting than one where we are not. Today SpaceX is actively developing the technologies to make this possible, with the ultimate goal of&nbsp;enabling human life on Mars.</p></div><h1 style="line-height: 1.2;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>DATA ENGINEER (STARLINK)</strong></span></h1> <p style="line-height: 1.2;">At SpaceX, we’re leveraging our experience building rockets and spacecraft to deploy Starlink, the world’s most advanced broadband internet system. Starlink is the world’s largest satellite constellation, delivering fast, reliable internet to more than 10 million users worldwide. We design, build, test, and operate the full stack — satellites, user terminals, and the software that ties it all together.</p> <p style="line-height: 1.2;">As a Data Engineer on the Starlink Enterprise team, you will improve the quality of data flowing into our commercial systems and build the integrations and AI agents that turn that cleaner data into better decisions and faster execution — from pipeline generation through closed revenue and ongoing enablement. You will partner with Enterprise sales, channel and reseller operations, sales ops, and enablement to fix messy inputs, reduce manual cleanup, and ship automations that make go-to-market workflows more reliable.</p> <h2 style="line-height: 1.2;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>RESPONSIBILITIES:</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li>Improve data hygiene and quality across go-to-market systems — identify bad, missing, duplicate, and stale records; implement validation, enrichment, and remediation so higher-quality data enters our system of record, opportunity, partner/reseller, and enablement workflows</li> <li>Raise the quality of inputs to AI agents and analytics so outputs (recommendations, deal status, enablement answers, reporting) are more accurate, trustworthy, and actionable</li> <li>Build integrations across internal tools and external systems so deal, customer, and partner context flows cleanly between sales, channel ops, and operational platforms — reducing manual re-entry and preventing quality issues at the source</li> <li>Develop Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers and related interfaces that connect AI assistants (including Grok) to internal and external go-to-market systems, with an emphasis on reliable, well-scoped access to the right data and tools</li> <li>Design, ship, and iterate AI agents that power Starlink Enterprise go-to-market workflows</li> <li>Contribute to AI-powered applications and automations that accelerate lead-to-revenue cycles, working within the additional datasets and systems architecture owned by the team</li> <li>Instrument data quality and agent performance with monitoring and feedback loops — catching regressions early and tying improvements to outcomes (e.g., cleaner pipeline, faster cycle times, higher adoption and trust)</li> <li>Partner closely with sales, sales ops, channel/reseller ops, and enablement to turn recurring data-quality problems and manual processes into durable automations and standards</li> <li>Create reusable tools, checks, and documentation that help the team maintain data quality and extend agents over time</li> </ul> <h2 style="line-height: 1.2;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>BASIC QUALIFICATIONS:</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li>Bachelor’s degree in computer science, data science, physics, mathematics, or another STEM discipline</li> <li>1+ years of professional data engineering or software engineering experience (internship experience applicable)</li> <li>1+ years of development experience in Python or another modern programming language (SQL, etc.)</li> </ul> <h2 style="line-height: 1.2;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE:</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience improving CRM data quality (deduping, enrichment, validation rules, field completeness, source-of-truth discipline)</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience building AI agents, LLM-powered applications, or tool-using assistants that integrate with real business systems (APIs, CRMs, ticketing, data warehouses)</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience designing APIs, webhooks, or integration layers that connect heterogeneous systems and keep data consistent across them</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Familiarity with Model Context Protocol (MCP) or similar patterns for exposing tools and data sources to AI systems</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience with CRM and go-to-market tooling; familiarity with Clay, HubSpot, Apollo, or similar sales engagement / enrichment platforms is a plus</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Professional experience supporting sales, sales ops, channel/reseller, or revenue operations workflows with data and automation</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience with SQL and modern data tooling; exposure to Grok, internal AI platforms, or comparable LLM developer tooling is a plus</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience with agent evaluation, guardrails, permissions, and human-in-the-loop patterns for production AI systems</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Ability to thrive in a fast-changing environment, take initiative on ambiguous problems, and develop new expertise as the product and stack evolve</li> </ul> <p><strong>ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p> <ul> <li data-uuid="2f22e95e-0037-4c59-860a-40599b2d0422">Must be available to work extended hours and weekends as needed</li> <li data-uuid="db0c2e6c-7390-4107-a4c3-dc07d01726b7">Willingness to travel to customer sites, other SpaceX locations, and events as needed</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>ITAR REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p> <ul> <li>To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR <a href="https://www.pmddtc.state.gov/?id=ddtc_kb_article_page&amp;sys_id=24d528fddbfc930044f9ff621f961987">here</a>. &nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>SpaceX is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with SpaceX is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.</p> <p>Applicants wishing to view a copy of SpaceX’s Affirmative Action Plan for veterans and individuals with disabilities, or applicants requiring reasonable accommodation to the application/interview process should reach out to&nbsp;<a href="mailto:EEOCompliance@spacex.com">EEOCompliance@spacex.com</a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p></div>

language (SQL, etc.)</li> </ul> <h2 style="line-height: 1.2;"><span style="font-size: 10pt;"><strong>PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE:</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience improving CRM data quality (deduping, enrichment, validation rules, field completeness, source-of-truth discipline)</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience building AI agents, LLM-powered applications, or tool-using assistants that integrate with real business systems (APIs, CRMs, ticketing, data warehouses)</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience designing APIs, webhooks, or integration layers that connect heterogeneous systems and keep data consistent across them</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Familiarity with Model Context Protocol (MCP) or similar patterns for exposing tools and data sources to AI systems</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience with CRM and go-to-market tooling; familiarity with Clay, HubSpot, Apollo, or similar sales engagement / enrichment platforms is a plus</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Professional experience supporting sales, sales ops, channel/reseller, or revenue operations workflows with data and automation</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience with SQL and modern data tooling; exposure to Grok, internal AI platforms, or comparable LLM developer tooling is a plus</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Experience with agent evaluation, guardrails, permissions, and human-in-the-loop patterns for production AI systems</li> <li style="line-height: 1.2;">Ability to thrive in a fast-changing environment, take initiative on ambiguous problems, and develop new expertise as the product and stack evolve</li> </ul> <p><strong>ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p> <ul> <li data-uuid="2f22e95e-0037-4c59-860a-40599b2d0422">Must be available to work extended hours and weekends as needed</li> <li data-uuid="db0c2e6c-7390-4107-a4c3-dc07d01726b7">Willingness to travel to customer sites, other SpaceX locations, and events as needed</li> </ul> <strong>COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS:</strong><br><br> Pay Range:<br> Level 1: $125,000.00 - $150,000.00<br>Level 2: $145,000.00 - $175,000.00<br><br> <p>Your actual level and base salary will be determined on a case-by-case basis and may vary based on the following considerations: job-related knowledge and skills, education, and experience.</p> <p>Base salary is just one part of your total rewards package at SpaceX. You may also be eligible for long-term incentives, in the form of company stock or long-term cash awards, as well as potential discretionary bonuses and the ability to purchase additional stock at a discount through an Employee Stock Purchase Plan. You will also receive access to comprehensive medical, vision, and dental coverage, access to a 401(k) retirement plan, short and long-term disability insurance, life insurance, paid parental leave, and various other discounts and perks. You may also accrue 3 weeks of paid vacation and will be eligible for 10 or more paid holidays per year. Employees accrue paid sick leave pursuant to Company policy which satisfies or exceeds the accrual, carryover, and use requirements of the law.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>ITAR REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p> <ul> <li>To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. title: Data Engineer (Starlink Network Analytics, Wi-Fi)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>SpaceX was founded under the belief that a future where humanity is out exploring the stars is fundamentally more exciting than one where we are not. Today SpaceX is actively developing the technologies to make this possible, with the ultimate goal of&nbsp;enabling human life on Mars.</p></div><p><strong>DATA ENGINEER (STARLINK NETWORK ANALYTICS, WIFI)</strong></p> <p>At SpaceX, we’re leveraging our experience in building rockets and spacecraft to deploy Starlink, the world’s most advanced broadband internet system. Starlink is the world’s largest satellite constellation and is providing fast, reliable internet to millions of users worldwide. We design, build, test, and operate all parts of the system – thousands of satellites, consumer receivers that allow users to connect within minutes of unboxing, and the software that brings it all together. We’ve only begun to scratch the surface of Starlink’s potential global impact and are looking for best-in-class engineers to help maximize Starlink’s utility for communities and businesses around the globe.&nbsp;</p> <p>As a Data Engineer, you will&nbsp;be responsible for&nbsp;developing the strategy, key metrics, tools, software services, and processes for assessing how well key aspects of Starlink are scaling and how effective the Starlink Network is itself, in serving millions of users around the globe. You will work with operators, subsystem responsible engineers, software engineers, and network engineers inside the Starlink organization as well as key contacts with various major external partners to help ensure the growth of this program.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>RESPONSIBILITIES:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Build and maintain mission-critical infrastructure, tools, processes, and custom software to objectively assess growth areas for the Starlink program</li> <li>Automate the aggregation of metrics and detection of widespread application issues across Starlink network, including WiFi&nbsp;</li> <li>Lead technical investigations about chronic application-level issues</li> <li>Build ground-based software systems that ingest, transform, and store data</li> <li>Apply data analytics, models, and techniques to data products created by space vehicles</li> <li>Fuse data from multiple sources to create usable information</li> </ul> <p><strong>BASIC QUALIFICATIONS:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Bachelor’s degree in computer science, data science, physics, mathematics, or a STEM discipline</li> <li>1+ years of data/software engineering experience (internship experience is applicable)</li> <li>Development experience in an object-oriented programming language (i.e. C, C++, Python)&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience in networking technologies and WiFi protocols (TCP/UDP, 802.11, etc)&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience building predictive models and machine learning pipelines on real world data (clustering analysis, prediction, anomaly detection) &nbsp;</li> <li>Experience in custom ETL design, implementation, and maintenance &nbsp;</li> <li>Experience handling large (TB+) datasets &nbsp;</li> <li>Experience with developing and deploying tools used for data analysis&nbsp;</li> <li>Ability to work effectively in a dynamic environment that includes working with changing needs and requirements&nbsp;</li> <li>Ability to take on projects that require taking initiative and developing new expertise&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Must be willing to work extended hours and weekends as needed</li> </ul> <strong>COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS:</strong><br><br> Pay Range:<br> Level 1: $125,000 - $150,000<br>Level 2: $145,000 - $175,000<br><br> <p>Your actual level and base salary will be determined on a case-by-case basis and may vary based on the following considerations: job-related knowledge and skills, education, and experience.</p> <p>Base salary is just one part of your total rewards package at SpaceX. You may also be eligible for long-term incentives, in the form of company stock or long-term cash awards, as well as potential discretionary bonuses and the ability to purchase additional stock at a discount through an Employee Stock Purchase Plan. You will also receive access to comprehensive medical, vision, and dental coverage, access to a 401(k) retirement plan, short and long-term disability insurance, life insurance, paid parental leave, and various other discounts and perks. You may also accrue 3 weeks of paid vacation and will be eligible for 10 or more paid holidays per year. Employees in Washington State accrue paid sick time in compliance with state and federal law. Company shuttles are offered to employees for roundtrip travel from select Seattle locations to the SpaceX Redmond office Monday to Friday.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>ITAR REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p> <ul> <li>To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR <a href="https://www.pmddtc.state.gov/?id=ddtc_kb_article_page&amp;sys_id=24d528fddbfc930044f9ff621f961987">here</a>. &nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>SpaceX is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with SpaceX is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.</p> <p>Applicants wishing to view a copy of SpaceX’s Affirmative Action Plan for veterans and individuals with disabilities, or applicants requiring reasonable accommodation to the application/interview process should reach out to&nbsp;<a href="mailto:EEOCompliance@spacex.com">EEOCompliance@spacex.com</a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p></div>

title: Data Engineer (Starship)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>SpaceX was founded under the belief that a future where humanity is out exploring the stars is fundamentally more exciting than one where we are not. Today SpaceX is actively developing the technologies to make this possible, with the ultimate goal of&nbsp;enabling human life on Mars.</p></div><p><strong><span data-contrast="auto">DATA ENGINEER (STARSHIP)</span></strong><span data-ccp-props="{&quot;201341983&quot;:0,&quot;335559739&quot;:0,&quot;335559740&quot;:240}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p>The Starship Supply Chain team is building the machine that builds the machine. We are engineering a supply chain to feed Starfactory and support thousands of Starship launches per year. Our team spans planning, procurement, supplier development, quality, material flow, and inventory.</p> <p>At the heart of this effort is the Data and Operations Engineering team. We build data pipelines, improve processes, and develop tools and automation that inform, accelerate, and focus the organization.</p> <p>Aerospace experience is not required to succeed here. Instead, we look for smart, motivated, and collaborative engineers who love solving problems and want to make a meaningful impact on an inspiring mission. You will own challenging problems end-to-end, working alongside a team of enthusiastic engineers to design and deliver solutions that help SpaceX move toward its goals at a rapid pace.</p> <p><strong><span data-contrast="auto">RESPONSIBILITIES:</span></strong></p> <ul> <li>Be the extreme owner of automations, applications, and data pipelines</li> <li>Own projects throughout their entire lifecycle: problem identification, solution ideation, architecting, implementation, adoption, monitoring, and iteration</li> <li>Query, clean, and transform data to inform and focus all levels of supply chain—from individual contributors to senior executives.</li> <li>Collaborate with central software teams on a shared vision for the software ecosystem, project requirements, roadmaps, and implementation details.</li> <li>Develop, standardize, and continuously improve key process indicators and other operational metrics</li> </ul> <p><strong><span data-contrast="auto">BASIC orchestration tools (e.g., Airflow, Prefect, or dbt)</li> <li>Programming experience in Python, C#, or similar languages</li> <li>Experience with data analysis and machine learning libraries such as Pandas, NumPy, and PyTorch</li> <li>Experience with data visualization and business intelligence tools (e.g., Power BI, Grafana, Metabase, or Tableau)</li> <li>Experience developing web apps, dashboards, or self-service platforms for non-technical stakeholders</li> <li>Experience with version control (Git), CI/CD, testing, and monitoring</li> <li>Strong understanding of data quality, statistical analysis, and data visualization best practices</li> </ul> <p><strong><span data-contrast="auto">ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:</span></strong><span title: Sr. Data Engineer, Ground Network Engineering (Business Analytics)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>SpaceX was founded under the belief that a future where humanity is out exploring the stars is fundamentally more exciting than one where we are not. Today SpaceX is actively developing the technologies to make this possible, with the ultimate goal of&nbsp;enabling human life on Mars.</p></div><p><strong>SR. DATA ENGINEER, GROUND NETWORK ENGINEERING (BUSINESS ANALYTICS)</strong></p> <p>At SpaceX, we are leveraging our experience in building rockets and spacecraft to deploy Starlink, the world’s most advanced broadband internet system. Starlink is the world’s largest satellite constellation and is providing fast, reliable internet to millions of users worldwide. We design, build, test, and operate all parts of the system – thousands of satellites, consumer receivers that allow users to connect within minutes of unboxing, and the software that brings it all together. We’ve only begun to scratch the surface of Starlink’s potential global impact and are looking for best-in-class engineers and analysts to rapidly accelerate our growth around the globe.</p> <p>As a Sr. Data Engineer for the Ground Network, you will play a critical role in building the framework that allow us to make decisions for our critical infrastructure. You will be defining and measuring infrastructure KPIs, analyzing data to extract key insights and identifying opportunities to improve spending in the ground network.</p> <p><strong>RESPONSIBILITIES</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Define and develop KPIs and associated metrics&nbsp;that quantify the&nbsp;cost&nbsp;of every asset of the Starlink Ground Network globally including gateway sites, points of presence, data centers, construction, fiber, power, land, hardware, etc.</li> <li>Conduct deep-dive analyses of Starlink ground infrastructure data to identify trends, uncover opportunities for improving&nbsp;cost.</li> <li>Regularly prepare reports and presentations that summarize findings, provide actionable insights, and track the effectiveness of implemented strategies.</li> <li>Build and maintain forecasting engines to predict cost of the Starlink Ground Network.</li> <li>Support&nbsp;cost&nbsp;analysis for launch of future markets.</li> <li>Refactor and scale data ingestion and delivery of cost systems for geographic expansion.</li> <li>Maintain and enhance the integrity and reliability of internal and external data sources. Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders including engineering, and external processors to capture additional data and to ensure data accuracy and consistency.</li> <li>Collaborate closely with software engineering and other cross functional teams.</li> <li>Architect backend services, APIs, and workflows to integrate planning, forecasting, deployments, and cost.</li> </ul> <p><strong>BASIC QUALIFICATIONS</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Bachelor’s degree in engineering, computer science, or related field</li> <li>5+ years of professional experience in reporting and analytics in network infrastructure.</li> <li>3+ years of experience with SQL.</li> </ul> <p><strong>PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Experience with inventory systems, logistics workflows, and/or supply chain tooling.</li> <li>Proficient in C#, C++, or Angular</li> <li>Basic understanding of Kubernetes concepts, including pods, services, deployments, and namespaces</li> <li>Prior success in delivering strong data visualizations using tools such as PowerBI, Grafana, Metabase or other real-time metrics systems</li> <li>Experience building backend services using APIs (Celery, FastAPI, Django, or similar frameworks) and system-to-system data exchange in logistics and supply chain environments</li> <li>Proven track record developing custom forecasting tools and planning logic</li> <li>Knowledge of MRP/ERP, WMS, or other supply chain systems</li> <li>Strong problem-solving skills and comfort working in ambiguous environments with evolving requirements</li> <li>Excellent communication and cross-functional program management skills&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Must be able to work extended hours or weekends</li> </ul> <p><strong>COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS:&nbsp;&nbsp; &nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Pay Range: &nbsp; &nbsp;<br>Network Engineer/Senior: $160,000.00 - $225,000.00/per year &nbsp; &nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p>Your actual level and base salary will be determined on a case-by-case basis and may vary based on the following considerations: job-related knowledge and skills, education, and experience.</p> <p>Base salary is just one part of your total rewards package at SpaceX. You may also be eligible for long-term incentives, in the form of company stock, stock options, or long-term cash awards, as well as potential discretionary bonuses and the ability to purchase additional stock at a discount through an Employee Stock Purchase Plan. You will also receive access to comprehensive medical, vision, and dental coverage, access to a 401(k) retirement plan, short and long-term disability insurance, life insurance, paid parental leave, and various other discounts and perks. You may also accrue 3 weeks of paid vacation and will be eligible for 10 or more paid holidays per year. Employees in Washington State accrue paid sick time in compliance with state and federal law. Company shuttles are offered to employees for roundtrip travel from select Seattle locations to the SpaceX Redmond office Monday to Friday.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>ITAR REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p> <ul> <li>To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR <a href="https://www.pmddtc.state.gov/?id=ddtc_kb_article_page&amp;sys_id=24d528fddbfc930044f9ff621f961987">here</a>. &nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>SpaceX is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with SpaceX is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.</p> <p>Applicants wishing to view a copy of SpaceX’s Affirmative Action Plan for veterans and individuals with disabilities, or applicants requiring reasonable accommodation to the application/interview process should reach out to&nbsp;<a href="mailto:EEOCompliance@spacex.com">EEOCompliance@spacex.com</a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p></div>

title: Sr. Software Engineer (Data Science/Data Engineering)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>SpaceX was founded under the belief that a future where humanity is out exploring the stars is fundamentally more exciting than one where we are not. Today SpaceX is actively developing the technologies to make this possible, with the ultimate goal of&nbsp;enabling human life on Mars.</p></div><p><strong>SR. SOFTWARE ENGINEER (DATA SCIENCE/DATA ENGINEERING)</strong></p> <p>The application software team is the central nervous system of SpaceX – we create mission critical applications that are used throughout SpaceX to accelerate launch vehicle production and flights as well as systems that allow Starlink to grow into a worldwide fast, reliable internet service.<span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span>We are looking for engineers who treat fellow teammates with fairness, respect, and support.<span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p>Our team is creating systems to analyze data from events like rocket launches or factory production workloads to always-on telemetry from the Starlink satellite constellation with point-in-time analysis, historical analysis and machine learning. We work closely with engineers throughout the company to create and update our systems with respect to crewed launches, Starship flights, changes to the Starlink network and much more.<span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p>Aerospace experience is not required to be successful here - rather we look for smart, motivated, collaborative engineers who love solving problems and want to make an impact on a super inspiring mission. You will have full ownership of challenging problems, working with a team of enthusiastic engineers to design and produce solutions that enable SpaceX to move towards our goals at a rapid pace. The success of the missions at SpaceX depends on the software that you and your team produce.<span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span data-contrast="auto"><strong>RESPONSIBILITIES:</strong></span><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li>Develop highly reliable and scalable data analysis libraries, systems, and data pipelines, to empower engineers across SpaceX</li> <li>Create new applications that improve how the business at SpaceX operates</li> <li>Collaborate with peers on architecture, design, and code reviews</li> <li>Build prototypes to prove out key design concepts and quantify technical constraints</li> <li>Own all aspects of software engineering and product development</li> <li>Deep dive into engineering problems and involve in corporation/coordination with domain experts</li> </ul> <p><strong>BASIC QUALIFICATIONS:</strong><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li>Bachelor's degree in computer science, engineering, math, or scientific discipline and 5+ years of professional experience in software development; OR 7+ years of professional experience building software in lieu of a degree</li> <li>3+ years of professional experience in full-stack development</li> </ul> <p><strong>PREFERRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE:</strong><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li>3+ years of professional experience in data engineering or data science</li> <li>2+ years of hands-on experience applying ML techniques (ex: CNNs, RNNs) to datasets</li> <li>Programming experience in Python, C#, or similar languages</li> <li>Experience working with data analysis libraries such as Python Pandas</li> <li>Experience with machine learning libraries like SciPi and PyTorch</li> <li>Experience with data exploration tools like Grafana, Jupyter Notebooks, Metabase, PowerBI or similar</li> <li>Experience with relational databases such as PostgreSQL</li> <li>Experience applying computer vision libraries such as OpenCV and Ultralytics to real-world problems</li> <li>Goodunderstanding of&nbsp;machine learning algorithms,&nbsp;linear algebra, calculus, probability, statistics, and optimization methods.<span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></li> <li>Good understanding of version control, testing, continuous integration, build, deployment and monitoring</li> <li>Some front-end experience in Angular, React, or similar JavaScript framework</li> </ul> <p><strong>ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS: </strong><span data-ccp-props="{}">&nbsp;</span></p> <ul> <li><span data-contrast="auto"> You may be asked to work extended hours/weekends dependent on launch cadence </span></li> <li>Must be willing to travel when needed</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>ITAR REQUIREMENTS:</strong></p> <ul> <li>To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR <a href="https://www.pmddtc.state.gov/?id=ddtc_kb_article_page&amp;sys_id=24d528fddbfc930044f9ff621f961987">here</a>. &nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>SpaceX is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with SpaceX is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.</p> <p>Applicants wishing to view a copy of SpaceX’s Affirmative Action Plan for veterans and individuals with disabilities, or applicants requiring reasonable accommodation to the application/interview process should reach out to&nbsp;<a href="mailto:EEOCompliance@spacex.com">EEOCompliance@spacex.com</a><em>.&nbsp;</em></p></div>

title: Senior Data Engineer – Hardware & Supply Chain
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Our mission at Oura is to empower every person to own their inner potential. Our award-winning products help our global community gain a deeper knowledge of their readiness, activity, and sleep quality by using their Oura Ring and its connected app. We've helped millions of people understand and improve their health by providing daily insights and practical steps to inspire healthy lifestyles.</p> <p>Empowering the world starts with living <a href="https://ouraring.com/about-us">our values</a> and empowering our team. As a quickly growing company focused on helping people live healthier and happier lives, we ensure that our team members have what they need to do their best work — both in and out of the office.&nbsp;</p></div><p>We are looking for a Senior Data Engineer – Hardware &amp; Supply Chain (Manufacturing) to join our Data Engineering &amp; Analytics team. Your primary focus will be building the data infrastructure that powers Oura's hardware manufacturing operations — including production yield, device traceability, quality metrics, and supply chain data. Oura's hardware data is growing rapidly and is currently supported by a single engineer, creating significant risk and limiting our ability to scale. While HW/SC Manufacturing data engineering is your core domain, we expect and actively support team members growing into adjacent areas — such as quality testing analytics, firmware data, or broader platform engineering — as the team evolves.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What you will do:</strong></p> <p><strong>Primary Focus – Hardware &amp; Manufacturing Data Engineering</strong></p> <p>- Design, build, and maintain data pipelines that ingest and process manufacturing data from Oura's hardware production processes — including device yield, assembly, traceability, and supply chain events.</p> <p>- Build data models that give Operations, Hardware, and Supply Chain teams reliable, timely visibility into production performance and supply health.</p> <p>- Integrate data from manufacturing execution systems (MES), ERP/supply chain platforms, and factory data sources into Oura's lakehouse.</p> <p>- Own end-to-end data quality and reliability for Hardware/SC data — from factory ingestion to analytics-ready tables.</p> <p>- Partner closely with Hardware Engineering, Operations, and Quality teams to understand data needs and deliver scalable data products.</p> <p>- Ensure manufacturing data systems meet security, compliance, and data governance requirements.</p> <p>- Contribute to reducing single-point-of-failure risk in Hardware data infrastructure.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Cross-functional Growth Areas (expected over time)</strong></p> <p>- Expand data engineering support to Quality Testing and firmware data domains</p> <p>- Contribute to broader lakehouse architecture using Apache Iceberg, Databricks, and dbt.</p> <p>- Support the Data Mesh initiative by establishing HW/SC as an owned data domain.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>We would love to have you on our team if you have:</strong></p> <p>- 5+ years of experience in data engineering, with exposure to manufacturing, operations, or supply chain data.</p> <p>- Strong SQL and Python skills with a track record of building reliable production pipelines.</p> <p>- Experience integrating data from manufacturing systems — MES, ERP (e.g., SAP, Oracle), factory IoT, or supply chain platforms.</p> <p>- Ability to model manufacturing data: production lots, device traceability, yield events, and supply chain transactions.</p> <p>- Experience running production data systems on AWS or equivalent cloud.</p> <p>- Self-motivated, pragmatic, and able to manage your own work in a distributed team.</p> <p>- Ability to collaborate effectively with Hardware and Operations stakeholders who may not have a data background.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What Makes You Stand Out</strong></p> <p>- Hands-on experience with hardware manufacturing data at a consumer electronics or IoT company.</p> <p>- Experience with device traceability, serialized manufacturing, or supply chain event modeling.</p> <p>- Familiarity with Databricks, Apache Iceberg, or dbt.</p> <p>- Exposure to Quality Testing data systems or firmware/device telemetry data.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits</strong></p> <p>At Oura, we care about you and your well-being. Everyone here at Oura has a ring of their own and we are continually looking to improve employee health. What we offer:</p> <p>- Competitive salary and equity packages</p> <p>- Health, dental, vision insurance, and mental health resources</p> <p>- An Oura Ring of your own plus employee discounts for friends &amp; family</p> <p>- 20 days of paid time off plus 13 paid holidays plus 8 days of flexible wellness time off</p> <p>- Paid sick leave and parental leave</p> <p>Oura is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.</p> <ul> <li>Region 1 $172,550- $203,000</li> <li>Region 2 $158,950- $187,000</li> <li>Region 3 $147,900- $174,000</li> </ul> <p>A recruiter can determine your zones/tiers based on your US location.</p> <p>We are not considering candidates residing in the following states: Alaska (AK), Delaware (DE), Iowa (IA), Mississippi (MS), Missouri (MO), Nebraska (NE), South Dakota (SD), Vermont (VT), West Virginia (WV), and Wisconsin (WI)</p> <p><strong>Disclaimer: Beware of fake job offers!</strong> We've been alerted to scammers posing as ŌURA recruiters. Our jobs are listed only on the ŌURA Careers page and trusted job boards. We will never ask for personal information or payment upfront. Official offers are sent through Docusign after a verbal offer.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><div class="styles--2Kv6p" data-ui="careers-page-content"> <p>Oura is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Individuals seeking employment at Oura are considered without regard to age, ancestry, color, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, mental or physical disability, national origin, protected family care or medical leave status, race, religion (including beliefs and practices or the absence thereof), sexual orientation, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. We will not tolerate discrimination or harassment based on any of these characteristics.</p> <p>We will work to ensure individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment.</p> <p>Disclaimer: Beware of fake job offers!<br>We’ve been alerted to scammers posing as ŌURA recruiters, especially for remote roles. Please note:</p> <ul> <li>Our jobs are listed only on the ŌURA Careers page and trusted job boards.</li> <li>We will never ask for personal information like ID or payment for equipment upfront.</li> <li>Official offers are sent through Docusign after a verbal offer, not via text or email.</li> </ul> <p>Stay cautious and protect your personal details.</p> <p>To all recruitment agencies: Oura does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Oura employees, or any other organization's location. Oura is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.</p> </div></div>

title: Senior Data Engineer (US - East Coast)
full_description: <div class="content-intro"><p>Our mission at Oura is to empower every person to own their inner potential. Our award-winning products help our global community gain a deeper knowledge of their readiness, activity, and sleep quality by using their Oura Ring and its connected app. We've helped millions of people understand and improve their health by providing daily insights and practical steps to inspire healthy lifestyles.</p> <p>Empowering the world starts with living <a href="https://ouraring.com/about-us">our values</a> and empowering our team. As a quickly growing company focused on helping people live healthier and happier lives, we ensure that our team members have what they need to do their best work — both in and out of the office.&nbsp;</p></div><p>We are looking for a Data Engineer – Databricks Support/Admin to join our Data Engineering &amp; Analytics team. In this role, your primary focus will be administering, stabilizing, and scaling our unified Databricks environment across all Oura business domains. While Databricks platform ownership is your core responsibility, we expect and actively support team members growing into adjacent data engineering areas — including pipeline development, data modeling, and cross-domain data mesh support — as the team and company scale.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>This is a remote role supporting US East Coast hours, with occasional in-person gatherings.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What you will do:</strong></p> <p><strong>Primary Focus – Databricks Platform Administration</strong></p> <p>- Own day-to-day administration, configuration, and health of Oura's global Databricks environment, serving stakeholders across IT, business, and data science domains.</p> <p>- Manage workspace governance: access controls, cluster policies, cost monitoring, job scheduling, and security configurations.</p> <p>- Support onboarding of new teams and use cases onto the Databricks platform as Oura unifies data across Finance, SDM, Commerce, Marketing, and other domains.</p> <p>- Proactively identify and resolve performance bottlenecks, reliability issues, and cost inefficiencies.</p> <p>- Maintain SLAs for platform availability and incident response across global time zones.</p> <p>- Partner with IT and Data Architecture teams to align platform configurations with Databricks best practices and Oura's Data Mesh approach.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Cross-functional Growth Areas (expected over time)</strong></p> <p>- Contribute to data pipeline development and Spark workload optimization.</p> <p>- Support data engineers working across domain-specific initiatives (Finance, Membership, HW/SC, etc.).</p> <p>- Assist with CI/CD tooling, infrastructure-as-code, and platform automation.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>We would love to have you on our team if you have:</strong></p> <p>- 5+ years of experience in data engineering or platform/infrastructure engineering.</p> <p>- Hands-on Databricks administration experience: workspace management, cluster configuration, access control (Unity Catalog preferred), and job orchestration.</p> <p>- Strong understanding of Apache Spark and distributed compute fundamentals.</p> <p>- Experience running production data systems on AWS (S3, Glue, Kinesis, IAM) or equivalent cloud platforms.</p> <p>- Familiarity with workflow orchestration tools and CI/CD practices.</p> <p>- Ability to support and communicate with non-technical stakeholders across multiple business domains.</p> <p>- Self-motivated and comfortable operating with autonomy in a distributed team environment.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What Makes You Stand Out</strong></p> <p>- Databricks Certified Administrator or equivalent certification.</p> <p>- Experience with Unity Catalog and data governance at scale.</p> <p>- Familiarity with Data Mesh principles and domain-oriented platform design.</p> <p>- Experience in a high-growth, multi-stakeholder data environment.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>At Oura, we care about you and your well-being. Everyone here at Oura has a ring of their own and we are continually looking to improve employee health.</p> <p>What we offer:</p> <ul> <li>Competitive salary and equity packages</li> <li>Health, dental, vision insurance, and mental health resources</li> <li>An Oura Ring of your own plus employee discounts for friends &amp; family</li> <li>20 days of paid time off plus 13 paid holidays plus 8 days of flexible wellness time off</li> <li>Paid sick leave and parental leave</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>Oura takes a market-based approach to pay, which may vary depending on your location. US locations are categorized into tiers based on a cost of labor index for that geographic area. While most offers will be closer to the starting range, successful candidates' pay will be determined based on job-related skills, experience, qualifications, work location, internal peer equity, and market conditions.
